ref: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
text: Ahyɛse no Nyankopɔn bɔɔ sor nye asaase.

ref: The earth was barren, with no form of life; it was under a roaring ocean covered with darkness. But the Spirit of God was moving over the water. The First Day
text: Nna asaase da hɔ pan a onnyi bɔbea biara; na isum agye bun no enyi: na Nyankopɔn no sunsum butuw nsu no enyi

ref: God said, “I command light to shine!” And light started shining.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Ekan mbra: na ekan bae.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: Na Nyankopɔn hun kan no dɛ oye: na Nyankopɔn tseew kan no fii sum no ho.

ref: and named the light “Day” and the darkness “Night.” Evening came, then morning—that was the first day. The Second Day
text: Na Ɔfrɛɛ ekan no Adzekyee, na isum no Adzesaa. Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a odzi kan nye no.

ref: God said, “I command a dome to separate the water above it from the water below it.”
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Wɔmma wimu mbra nsu no finimfin, na wɔmma ɔntsew nsu no mu ebien: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Na Nyankopɔn frɛɛ wimu no ɔsor. Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia ebien nye no.

ref: God said, “I command the water under the sky to come together in one place, so there will be dry ground.” And that's what happened.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Wɔmma nsu a ɔwɔ sor n’ase no mpra nkɔboa fakor na wɔmma wewewdo nyi noho: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

ref: God named the dry ground “Land,” and he named the water “Ocean.” God looked at what he had done and saw that it was good.
text: Na Nyankopɔn frɛɛ wewewdo no Asaase; na nsu no no mboano no ɔfrɛɛ no Epo: na Nyankopɔn hun dɛ oye.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Asaase mfifir efuw, nhaban a wɔsow aba, na ndua a wɔsow nduaba dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tse, wɔ asaase do: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

ref: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Na asaase fifiir efuw, haban a ɔsow aba dɛ mbrɛ no su tse: na Nyankopɔn hun dɛ oye.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia ebiasa nye no.

ref: God said, “I command lights to appear in the sky and to separate day from night and to show the time for seasons, special days, and years.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Nkandzea mbra wimu mbeyi adzekyee mfi adzesaa ho: na ɔnyɛ nsɛnkyerɛdze, nye mbernkyekyɛmu, nye nda nye mfe:

ref: I command them to shine on the earth.” And that's what happened.
text: na ɔnyɛ nkandzea wɔ wimu nhyerɛn asaase do: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

ref: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
text: Na Nyankopɔn yɛɛ nkandzea akɛse ebien; kandzea kɛse no dɛ ɔnhwɛ adzekyee do, na kakraba no dɛ ɔnhwɛ adzesaa do: na ɔyɛɛ nworaba so.

ref: Then God put these lights in the sky to shine on the earth,
text: Na Nyankopɔn dze sisii wimu dɛ wɔnhyerɛn asaase do,

ref: to rule day and night, and to separate light from darkness. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: na wɔnhwɛ adzekyee nye adzesaa do, na wonyi kan mfi sum ho: na Nyankopɔn hun dɛ oye.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fourth day. The Fifth Day
text: Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia anan nye no.

ref: God said, “I command the ocean to be full of living creatures, and I command birds to fly above the earth.”
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Nsu no mu mbra mbowa ahorow pii na ntumbowa so nkyin asaase do wɔ sor.

ref: So God made the giant sea monsters and all the living creatures that swim in the ocean. He also made every kind of bird. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Na Nyankopɔn bɔɔ esumbowa akɛse no, nkakramba na ahorow pii, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tsetsee, na ntumbowa nyinara dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tsetsee: na Nyankopɔn hun dɛ oye.

ref: Then he gave the living creatures his blessing—he told the ocean creatures to increase and live everywhere in the ocean and the birds to increase everywhere on earth.
text: Na Nyankopɔn hyiraa hɔn see dɛ, Hom nwo, na hom ndɔr, na hom nhyɛ po mu nsu mu ma, na ntumbowa ndɔr wɔ asaase do.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fifth day. The Sixth Day
text: Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia enum nye no.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Asaase ndɔr mbowa a nkwa wɔ hɔn mu dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tsetsee, fiembowa, nye nketsebowa, nye asaase mbowadoma dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tsetsee: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

ref: God made every one of them. Then he looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Na Nyankopɔn yɛɛ asaase do mbowadoma dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tsetsee, na Nyankopɔn hun dɛ oye.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Wɔmma yɛnyɛ nyimpa wɔ hɛn su do, wɔ hɛn saso do: na wonya tum wɔ po mu nam, nye wimu ntumbowa, nye fiembowa, nye mbowa a wɔwea asaase do nyinara.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Nyankopɔn bɔɔ nyimpa wɔ nankasa no su do, Nyew, Nyankopɔn no su do na ɔbɔɔ nyimpa; ɔbɔɔ hɔn ɔbanyin nye ɔbaa.

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Na Nyankopɔn hyiraa hɔn: na Ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Hom nwo, na hom ndɔr, na hom nhyɛ asaase ma, na hom ndzi do tum. Hom nya tum wɔ po mu nam do, nye wimu ntumbowa do, nye asaase do mbowa nyinara do.

ref: I have provided all kinds of fruit and grain for you to eat.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Hwɛ, mama hom nduadzewa na nduaba a ɔwɔ asaase nyinara enyi, dɛ ɔnyɛ edziban mma hom:

ref: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.
text: na asaase do mbowadoma nyinara nye wimu ntumbowa nyinara dze, mama hɔn sar na nhaban nhaban dɛ wondzi: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

ref: God looked at what he had done. All of it was very good! Evening came, then morning—that was the sixth day.
text: Na Nyankopɔn hun adze a ɔayɛ nyinara, na hwɛ, odzi mu papaapa. Na adze sae na adzekyee, da a otsia esia nye no.

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Na wowiee sor nye asaase nye dza ɔwɔ mu nyinara yɛ.

ref: By the seventh day God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Na da a otsia esuon no nna Nyankopɔn ewie n’edwuma a ɔyɛɛ no nyinara; na ɔhomee fii no ndwuma no ho.

ref: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.
text: Na Nyankopɔn hyiraa da a otsia esuon no do na ɔtseew ho: osiandɛ ɔno mu na ɔhomee fii no ndwuma a ɔayɛ nyinara ho. Eden Ture

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Iyi nye sor nye asaase ho nsɛm. Ber a EWURADZE Nyankopɔn yɛɛ asaase nye sor no,

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: nna hamu dua biara nnyae mmbaa asaase do, na hamu ahaban biara nnyae mmfirii: osiandɛ EWURADZE Nyankopɔn nntɔɔ nsu nnguu asaase do, na nyimpa so nnyi hɔ a ɔbɔdɔw.

ref: But streams came up from the ground and watered the earth.
text: Na nsu fi asaase do bɔfɔɔw asaase enyi nyinara.

ref: The Lord God took some soil from the ground and made a man. God breathed life into the man, and the man started breathing.
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn dze asaase do huntuma bɔɔ nyimpa, na oguu nkwahwea guu n’ahwentokura mu; na nyimpa bɛyɛɛ ɔkra tseasefo.

ref: The Lord made a garden in a place called Eden, which was in the east, and he put the man there.
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn yɛɛ ture wɔ Eden, wɔ boka afamu; na hɔ na ɔdze nyimpa a ɔayɛ no no toe.

ref: The Lord God filled the garden with all kinds of beautiful trees and fruit trees. Two other trees were in the middle of the garden. One of these gave life—the other gave the wisdom to know the difference between right and wrong.
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn maa ndua a ɔyɛ fɛw, na oye dɛ wodzi nyinara fifirii. Ture no ne finimfin na nkwadua no wɔ, na papa nye bɔn ho nyimdzee dua no so si.

ref: From Eden a river flowed out to water the garden, then it divided into four rivers.
text: Na esutsen bi fi Eden kɔree kɔfɔɔw ture no; na ofi hɔ na no mu paapaae, na ɔbɛyɛɛ nsutsir anan.

ref: The first one is the Pishon River that flows through the land of Havilah,
text: Dza odzi kan no ne dzin dze Pison: ɔno na otwa Havila asaase a sika kɔkɔɔ wɔ do no nyinara ho hyia.

ref: where pure gold, rare perfumes, and precious stones are found.
text: Na asaase no do sika ye, na bedola eduhuam nye epopobibir abo so wɔ hɔ.

ref: The second is the Gihon River that winds through Ethiopia.
text: Na esutsen a otsia ebien no ne dzin dze Gihon: ɔno na otwa Kus asaase nyinara ho hyia no.

ref: The Tigris River that flows east of Assyria is the third, and the fourth is the Euphrates River.
text: Na esutsen a otsia ebiasa no ne dzin dze Tigris: ɔno na otwa mu wɔ Asiria boka no. Esutsen a otsia anan no nye Eufrates.

ref: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn faa nyimpa no, na ɔdze no kɔtoo Eden ture no mu dɛ onsiesie hɔ, na ɔnhwɛ hɔ.

ref: But the Lord told him, “You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Na Ɔhyɛɛ mbra maa nyimpa no dɛ, Ture yi mu ndua nyinara dzi bi:

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: na papa nye bɔn ho nyimdzee dua no dze, mma nndzi bi: na da ara a ibedzi bi no, ampaara dɛ ibowu.

ref: The Lord God said, “It isn't good for the man to live alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.”
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Onnye dɛ nyimpa no nko tsena hɔ; mebɛyɛ boafo a ɔfata no ama no.

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn maa nda heii tɔɔ nyimpa no do, na ɔdae; na oyii ne mparba mu kor, na ɔdze nam hyɛɛ n’ananmu.

ref: the Lord made a woman out of the rib. The Lord God brought her to the man,
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn dze aparba a oyii no nyimpa no mu no yɛɛ ɔbaa, na ɔdze no brɛɛ nyimpa no.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Na nyimpa no see dɛ, Nyew! Me mbiew mu biew, na mo honam mu nam ankasa nye yi. Oyi dze, wɔbɛfrɛ no Ɔbaa, osiandɛ woyii no wɔ Banyin mu.

ref: That's why a man will leave his own father and mother. He marries a woman, and the two of them become like one person.
text: Dɛm ntsi na banyin begya ne na nye n’egya wɔ hɔ, na ɔdze noho akɛbata ne yer ho: na wɔayɛ honankor.

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: Nna hɔn beenu no nyinara dzi adagyaw, banyin no nye ne yer no, naaso wɔammfɛr.

ref: The snake was sneakier than any of the other wild animals that the Lord God had made. One day it came to the woman and asked, “Did God tell you not to eat fruit from any tree in the garden?”
text: Nna abowatsena no yɛ onyitsewfo kyɛn hamu abowa biara a EWURADZE Nyankopɔn ayɛ. Na obisaa ɔbaa no dɛ, Aso ampaara dɛ Nyankopɔn ase hom dɛ, Mma hom nndzi ture yi mu ndua nyinara bi?

ref: The woman answered, “God said we could eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Na ɔbaa no see abowatsena no dɛ, Ture yi mu ndua n’eduaba no yɛwɔ ho kwan dɛ yedzi bi;

ref: except the one in the middle. He told us not to eat fruit from that tree or even to touch it. If we do, we will die.”
text: mbom dua a osi ture yi ne finimfin no n’eduaba no na Nyankopɔn ase dɛ, Mma yenndzi, nkyii so mma yɛmmfa hɛn nsa nnka, amma yeennwuwu.

ref: “No, you won't!” the snake replied.
text: Na abowatsena no buaa ɔbaa no dɛ, Hom nnkowu bi:

ref: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”
text: osiandɛ Nyankopɔn nyim dɛ da a hom bedzi bi no, hom enyi bobue, na hom ayɛ dɛ Nyankopɔn na hom ehu papa nye bɔn.

ref: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
text: Na ɔbaa no hun dɛ dua no ye dɛ wodzi, na ɔyɛ enyiwa do fɛw, yɛ akɔndɔ, na otum so dɛ obue enyi no, ɔtseew n’aba no bi dzii; na ɔmaa no kun a ɔka no ho no so bi, na odzii.

ref: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
text: Na hɔn beenu no nyinara hɔn enyiwa buebuei, na wohun dɛ wodzi adagyaw; na wɔpempaam adɔmbadua n’ahataw, na wɔyɛɛ nsamenyim maa hɔnho.

ref: Late in the afternoon, when the breeze began to blow, the man and woman heard the Lord God walking in the garden. So they hid behind some trees. Sin Brings a Curse
text: Na ewimbir no, wɔtsee EWURADZE Nyankopɔn ne nka dɛ ɔnam ture no mu, na Adam nye ne yer no sumae fii EWURADZE Nyankopɔn n’enyim wɔ ture no mu ndua no mu.

ref: The Lord God called out to the man and asked, “Where are you?”
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn frɛɛ banyin no na obisaa no dɛ, Ewɔ hen?

ref: The man answered, “I was naked, and when I heard you walking through the garden, I was frightened and hid!”
text: Na banyin no buaa dɛ, Metsee wo ndze wɔ ture no mu, na musuroi, osiandɛ midzi adagyaw; na musumaa moho.

ref: “How did you know you were naked?” God asked. “Did you eat any fruit from that tree in the middle of the garden?”
text: Na obisaa no dɛ, Woana kaa kyerɛɛ wo dɛ idzi adagyaw? Ana edzi dua a mehyɛɛ wo ho mbra dɛ mma nndzi bi no bi?

ref: “It was the woman you put here with me,” the man said. “She gave me some of the fruit, and I ate it.”
text: Na banyin no see dɛ, ɔbaa no a edze no bɛkaa mo ho no, ɔno na ɔmaa me dua no bi, na midzii.

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn bisaa ɔbaa no dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ayɛ yi? Na ɔbaa no see dɛ, Abowatsena no na ɔdaadaa me ma midzii.

ref: So the Lord God said to the snake: “Because of what you have done, you will be the only animal to suffer this curse— For as long as you live, you will crawl on your stomach and eat dirt.
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn see abowatsena no dɛ, Osiandɛ ayɛ iyi ntsi, wɔadom wo wɔ fiembowa nyinara mu, na hamu mbowadoma nyinara mu; wo yafun do na ebɛnantsew, na huntuma na ibedzi wo nkwa nda nyinara.

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: Medze ɔtan mobɔto ɔwo nye ɔbaa no ntamu, na w’asefo nye n’asefo ntamu: ɔbɔbɔ wo tsir, na wo so asɔw n’anantsir.

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Ɔsee ɔbaa no dɛ, Mebɛma w’awoko mu amandzehu adɔɔ so kɛse; ɔfona mu na ebɔwo mba; na ebɛdan wo kun, na ɔbɔhwɛ wo do.

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: Na ɔsee Adam dɛ, Osiandɛ etsie wo yer ne ndze, na edzi dua a mehyɛɛ wo ho mbra dɛ, Mma nndzi bi no bi ntsi, wɔadom asaase wɔ ɔwo ntsi; ɔfona mu na ibedzidzi do wo nkwa nda nyinara;

ref: Your food will be plants, but the ground will produce thorns and thistles.
text: nhwirom nye nsoɛ so na obefifir ama wo; na ibedzi hamu ahaban;

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: w’enyim mfifir mu na ibedzidzi kɛpem dɛ ebɛsan akɔ daadze; osiandɛ ɔno mu na woyii wo fii: na huntuma nye wo, na huntuma mu na ebɛsan akɔ.

ref: The man Adam named his wife Eve because she would become the mother of all who live.
text: Na banyin no too ne yer no dzin Eve; osiandɛ ɔno na ɔyɛ atseasefo nyinara hɔn na.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn yɛɛ nwoma ntar maa Adam nye ne yer, na ɔdze hyɛɛ hɔn. Wɔpam Adam na Eve fi Ture no mu

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Hwɛ, nyimpa no abɛyɛ dɛ hɛn mu kor, ohu papa nye bɔn; na afei bɛyɛ na ɔatsen ne nsa, na ɔatsew nkwadua no so bi edzi, na ɔatsena ase daa;

ref: So the Lord God sent them out of the Garden of Eden, where they would have to work the ground from which the man had been made.
text: dɛm ntsi EWURADZE Nyankopɔn tuu nyimpa fii Eden ture no mu dɛ ɔnkɔdɔw daadze a woyii no fii mu no.

ref: Then God put winged creatures at the entrance to the garden and a flaming, flashing sword to guard the way to the life-giving tree.
text: Na oyii nyimpa no edzi; na ɔdze Kerubim, nye gya nkrantsee a onyinanyinam, sii Eden ture no enyim boka, dɛ wɔnweɔn nkwadua no ho kwan no.

ref: Adam and Eve had a son. Then Eve said, “I'll name him Cain because I got him with the help of the Lord.”
text: Na banyin no nye ne yer Eve dae na onyinsenii; na ɔwoo Kain, na ɔsee dɛ, Medze EWURADZE no mboa enya banyin.

ref: Later she had another son and named him Abel. Abel became a sheep farmer, but Cain farmed the land.
text: Na ɔwoo babanyin bio na ɔfrɛɛ no Abel. Nna Abel yɛɛ oguanhwɛfo, na Kain dze okuafo a.

ref: One day, Cain gave part of his harvest to the Lord,
text: Nda guu mu no, ɔbaa dɛ Kain dze ekuadze bɔbɔɔ EWURADZE afɔr,

ref: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,
text: na Abel so dze no nguan mu mbakan na dza wɔadodɔ seradze no mu bi bae. Na EWURADZE n’enyi sɔɔ Abel nye n’afɔrbɔ:

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: na Kain nye n’afɔrbɔ dze n’enyi annsɔ. Na Kain no bo fuuw kɛsenara, na omunae.

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
text: Na EWURADZE bisaa Kain dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na wo bo efuw yi? Na ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na emuna yi?

ref: If you had done the right thing, you would be smiling. But you did the wrong thing, and now sin is waiting to attack you like a lion. Sin wants to destroy you, but don't let it!
text: Sɛ eyɛɛ papa a nkyɛ w’enyim nnkɛba nyam? Na dɛ annyɛ papa ntsi, bɔn kotow w’abowano rohwehwɛ w’ekyir kwan naaso ɔwɔ dɛ ehyɛ wo ho do.

ref: Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let's go for a walk.” And when they were out in a field, Cain attacked and killed him.
text: Na Kain kaa kyerɛɛ no nua Abel dɛ ɔmma wɔnkɔ haban mu. Na ɔbaa dɛ wɔwɔ hamu no, Kain soɛɛr no nua Abel do, na okum no.

ref: Afterwards the Lord asked Cain, “Where is Abel?” “How should I know?” he answered. “Am I supposed to look after my brother?”
text: Na EWURADZE bisaa Kain dɛ, Wo nua Abel wɔ hen? Na Kain buaa dɛ, Minnyim: ana emi nye mo nua no hwɛdofo?

ref: Then the Lord said: Why have you done this terrible thing? You killed your own brother, and his blood flowed onto the ground. Now his blood is calling out for me to punish you.
text: Na EWURADZE see no dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ayɛ yi? Wo nua no bɔgyaa ne ndze fi famu keka mu frɛ me.

ref: And so, I'll put you under a curse. Because you killed Abel and made his blood run out on the ground, you will never be able to farm it again.
text: Na afei wofi asaase a ɔahan n’anomu regye wo nua no bɔgyaa wɔ wo nsamu no do wɔadom wo:

ref: When you try to farm the land, it won't produce anything for you. From now on, you'll be without a home, and you'll spend the rest of your life wandering from place to place.
text: Ofi ndɛ kɔ yi, sɛ edɔw asaase a, onnkeyi no ndɔbaa ama wo; na afei so ɔkobɔfo nye okyimakyimafo na ebɛyɛ wɔ asaase do.

ref: “This punishment is too hard!” Cain said.
text: Na Kain see EWURADZE dɛ, Mo bɔn ho akatua so kyɛn dɛ mubotum asoa.

ref: “You're making me leave my home and live far from you. I will have to wander about without a home, and just anyone could kill me.”
text: Hwɛ, ndɛ eyi me edzi wɔ asaase yi do; na mubosuma efi w’enyim; na mebɛyɛ kobɔfo nye kyimakyimafo wɔ asaase do; na ɔbɛba dɛ, obiara a obohu me no oboku me.

ref: “No!” the Lord answered. “Anyone who kills you will be punished seven times worse than I am punishing you.” So the Lord put a mark on Cain to warn everyone not to kill him.
text: Na EWURADZE see no dɛ wonnkoku wo, osiandɛ obiara a oboku Kain no wobedzi no wer mpɛn esuon. Na EWURADZE yɛɛ asɛnkyerɛdze bi maa Kain, amma obi hu no a, oeennku no. Kain Asefo

ref: But Cain had to go far from the Lord and live in the Land of Wandering, which is east of Eden. More and More People
text: Na Kain fii EWURADZE n’enyim kɔree, na ɔkɛtsenaa Nod asaase do wɔ Eden no boka.

ref: Later, Cain and his wife had a son named Enoch. At the time Cain was building a town, and so he named it Enoch after his son.
text: Na Kain nye ne yer dae: na onyinsenii, na ɔwoo Enok: na ɔkyekyeer kurow, na ɔfrɛɛ kurow no ne dzin Enok, dɛ mbrɛ ne ba no ne dzin tse.

ref: Then Enoch had a son named Irad, who had a son named Mehujael, who had a son named Methushael, who had a son named Lamech.
text: Na Enok woo Irad; na Irad woo Mehujael: na Mehujael woo Metusael: na Metusael woo Lamek.

ref: Lamech and Zillah had a son named Tubal Cain who made tools out of bronze and iron. They also had a daughter, whose name was Naamah.
text: Na Zila, so woo Tubal-kain a ɔton kɔɔber nye dadze ndzɛmba a wɔdze twa adze nyinara no; na Tubal-kain n’akyerɛbaa nye Naama.

ref: One day, Lamech said to his two wives, “A young man wounded me, and I killed him.
text: Na Lamek see ne yernom dɛ: Hom ntsie me ndze, Ada nye Zila; Hom nyɛ aso mma me kasaa, Lamek yernom: Na meku ɔbarimba dɛ opiraa me, Na meku aberantsɛ dɛ ɔboor me,

ref: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
text: Sɛ wobedzi Kain ho wer mpɛn esuon a, Ampa, Lamek dze, mpɛn eduosuon esuon.

ref: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
text: Na Adam nye ne yer daa bio; na ɔwoo babanyin, na ɔtoo no dzin Set: Na ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn dze ba fofor ahyɛ me Abel n’ananmu; osiandɛ Kain kum no.

ref: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.
text: Na Set so, wɔwoo babanyin maa no; na ɔtoo no dzin Enos: nna afei adasa hyɛɛ ase bɔɔ EWURADZE ne dzin sɔɔr no.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Na nda a Adam dzii nyinara ɔyɛ mfe ahaakron eduasa.

ref: and died at the age of
text: na Set ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron nye duebien.

ref: and died at the age of
text: na Enos ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron nye enum.

ref: and died at the age of
text: na Kenan ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron nye du: na owui.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Mahalalel ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaawɔtwe eduokron enum.

ref: and died at the age of
text: na Jared ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron eduosia ebien na owui.

ref: and God took him away at the age of
text: na Enok nye Nyankopɔn nantsewee: na woennhu no bio, osiandɛ Nyankopɔn faa no kɔree.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Metusela ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron eduosia akron: na owui.

ref: Lamech said, “I'll name him Noah because he will give us comfort, as we struggle hard to make a living on this land that the Lord has put under a curse.”
text: na ɔtoo no dzin Noa, dɛ, Oyiara na ɔbɛkyekye hɛn werɛ wɔ hɛn edwuma nye hɛn nsaano berɛ mu, osian asaase a EWURADZE apaa no no ntsi.

ref: and died at the age of
text: na Lamek ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaesuon eduosuon esuon.

ref: More and more people were born, until finally they spread all over the earth. Some of their daughters were so beautiful that supernatural beings came down and married the ones they wanted.
text: Nyankopɔn ne mba hun nyimpa mbabaa no dɛ hɔn ho yɛ fɛw; na wɔwarwaar hɔn mu dza wɔpɛ nyinara.

ref: Then the Lord said, “I won't let my life-giving breath remain in anyone forever. No one will live for more than
text: Na EWURADZE see dɛ, Mo sunsum nnkɛnye nyimpa edzi aper daa, osiandɛ ɔyɛ honam ara. Ofi dɛm ber yi ne nda bɛyɛ mfe ɔha eduonu.

ref: The children of the supernatural beings who had married these women became famous heroes and warriors. They were called Nephilim and lived on the earth at that time and even later.
text: Nda no mu no nna eso n’ekyir no, nna abran akɛse wɔ asaase do, osiandɛ ber a Nyankopɔn ne mba nye nyimpa mbabaa hyiae no, wɔwoo mba maa hɔn, woyinom nye tsetse etumfo no, nkorɔfo a wɔgyee dzin no.

ref: The Lord saw how bad the people on earth were and that everything they thought and planned was evil.
text: Na EWURADZE hun dɛ nyimpa n’emumuyɛ ayɛ kɛse wɔ asaase do, na dɛ n’akoma mu nsusui nyinara yɛ bɔn ara nko daa no,

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: EWURADZE nuu noho dɛ ɔyɛɛ nyimpa wɔ asaase do, na ɔyɛɛ no yaw wɔ n’akoma mu.

ref: and he said, “I'm going to destroy every person on earth! I'll even wipe out animals, birds, and reptiles. I'm sorry I ever made them.”
text: Na EWURADZE see dɛ, Mebɛsɛɛ nyimpa a mabɔ no no efi asaase enyi: nyew, nyimpa, nye mbowa nyinara; osiandɛ menu moho dɛ mayɛ hɔn.

ref: But the Lord was pleased with Noah,
text: Naaso Noa dze, onyaa enyimpa wɔ EWURADZE enyim. Noa Adaka No

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Iyinom nye Noa no ho asɛm. Nna Noa yɛ nyimpa tseneneenyi na odzi mu wɔ n’ebusuasantsen mu: Noa nye Nyankopɔn nantsewee.

ref: He had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text: Na Noa woo mbanyin baasa, Sem, Ham, nye Jafet.

ref: God knew that everyone was terribly cruel and violent.
text: Na Nyankopɔn hwɛɛ asaase, na, hwɛ, asɛɛ; osiandɛ nyimpa asɛɛ no kwan wɔ asaase do.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Fa dua pa pam hɛn ma woho; yɛ adan wɔ hɛn no mu, na fa ihua tar no mu nye n’ekyir.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Na Noa yɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ Nyankopɔn hyɛɛ no no nyinara pɛpɛɛpɛr.

ref: I'm going to send a flood that will destroy everything that breathes! Nothing will be left alive.
text: Na hwɛ, emi, medze nsuyirii reba asaase do abɛsɛɛ abɔdze a nkwahom wɔ mu nyinara efi sor ase: adze a ɔwɔ asaase do nyinara bowu.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Na menye wo bedzi ahyɛmu: na aba hɛn no mu, ɔwo, nye wo mba, nye wo yer, nye wo mba hɔn yernom ka wo ho.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Ntumbowa ahorow, fiembowa ahorow nye mbowa ahorow nyinara hɔn mu ebien ebien bra wo nkyɛn; ama woeenya nkwa.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na fa edziban nyinara bi ma woho, na boa ano kɔ wo nkyɛn; ama ɔaayɛ edziban ama ɔwo nye hɔn.

ref: The Lord told Noah: Take your whole family with you into the boat, because you are the only one on this earth who pleases me.
text: Na EWURADZE see Noa dɛ, ɔwo nye wo fie nyinara nkɔ hɛn no mu; osiandɛ ɔwo na mehu wo dɛ etsen wɔ m’enyim wɔ ber yi mu.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.
text: Mbowa a hɔn ho tsew nyinara fa hɔn mu esuon esuon, onyin nye ne ber; na mbowa a hɔn ho nntsew no mu ebien ebien, onyin na ne ber;

ref: Also take seven pairs of every kind of bird with you. Do this so there will always be animals and birds on the earth.
text: wimu ntumbowa mu so, esuon esuon, enyin nye aber; ama hɔn ase eenya nkwa wɔ asaase nyinara enyi.

ref: He obeyed God and took a male and a female of each kind of animal and bird into the boat with him.
text: ebien ebien kɔr Noa nkyɛn wɔ hɛn no mu, enyin nye aber, dɛ mbrɛ Nyankopɔn hyɛɛ Noa no.

ref: Seven days later a flood began to cover the earth.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ nda esuon ekyir no, nsuyirii no baa asaase do.

ref: The water under the earth started gushing out everywhere, the sky opened like windows, and rain poured down for
text: Na nsu tɔree guu asaase do adaduanan adzekyee nye adzesaa.

ref: On that day Noah and his wife went into the boat with their three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and their wives.
text: Da no ara na Noa nye ne yer na ne mba Sem, Ham, nye Jafet, hɔn yernom baasa no ka hɔn ho, kɔr hɛn no mu.

ref: They took along every kind of animal, tame and wild, including the birds.
text: Hɔn, nye mbowa ahorow, fiembowa na hambowa, nye ntumbowa ahorow nyinara kɔr hɛn no mu.

ref: Noah took a male and a female of every living creature with him,
text: Na wɔkɔr Noa nkyɛn wɔ hɛn no mu, honam a nkwahom wɔ mu nyinara mu ebien ebien,

ref: just as God had told him to do. And when they were all in the boat, the Lord closed the door.
text: enyin nye aber na wɔkɔr mu, dɛ mbrɛ Nyankopɔn hyɛɛ Noa no: na EWURADZE toow no mu.

ref: Finally, the mighty flood was so deep that even the highest mountain peaks were about seven meters below the surface of the water.
text: Nsu no boor do kɔr sor basafa esuon na fa; na nkokwaa do katae.

ref: Not a bird, animal, reptile, or human was left alive anywhere on earth.
text: Na biribiara a nkwa wɔ mu wɔ asaase do nyinara wui, ntumbowa, nye fiembowa, nye mbowadoma, nye nketsebowa a wɔwea asaase do nyinara, nye nyimpa nyinara.

ref: The Lord destroyed everything that breathed. Nothing was left alive except Noah and the others in the boat.
text: Na EWURADZE sɛɛ adze a nkwa wɔ mu a ɔwɔ asaase do no nyinara, nyimpa, fiembowa, nye nketsebowa, nye sor ntumbowa: na ɔkaa Noa, nye hɔn a wɔnye no wɔ hɛn no mu no.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: Na nsu no yirii wɔ asaase do nda ɔha eduonum.

ref: God did not forget about Noah and the animals with him in the boat. So God made a wind blow, and the water started going down.
text: Na Nyankopɔn kaa Noa, na adze a nkwa wɔ mu a wɔnye no wɔ hɛn no mu no nyinara: na Nyankopɔn maa mframa bɔe faa asaase do, na nsu no huamee;

ref: God stopped up the places where the water had been gushing out from under the earth. He also closed up the sky, and the rain stopped.
text: bun no no nsunyiwa so nye ɔsor ntokura ano sisiwii, na nsu a ofi sor gyaa tɔ;

ref: Then on the seventeenth day of the seventh month of the year, the boat came to rest somewhere in the Ararat mountains.
text: Na ehɛn no kesii Ararat nkokwaa do wɔ bosoom a otsia esuon no mu, bosoom no ne da a otsia duesuon no mu.

ref: The water kept going down, and the mountain tops could be seen on the first day of the tenth month.
text: Na nsu no kɔr do twee araa dze kodur bosoom a otsia du no mu no, bosoom no ne da a odzi kan no mu, na nkokwaa etsifi dedaa hɔ.

ref: Forty days later Noah opened a window to send out a raven, but it kept flying around until the water had dried up.
text: na ɔsomaa akonkoran, na ofiir edzi kekyinii araa ma ɔdze kɛpeem dɛ nsu wewewee fii asaase do.

ref: Noah wanted to find out if the water had gone down, so he sent out a dove.
text: Na ɔsomaa ebubur fii ne nkyɛn, dɛ ɔnkɔhwɛ kyerɛ nsu no ehuam efi asaase enyi;

ref: Deep water was still everywhere, and when the dove could not find a place to land, it flew back to the boat. Then Noah held out his hand and helped it back in.
text: na ebubur no ennya ne nan sibew, na ɔsanee baa no hɔ wɔ hɛn no mu, osiandɛ nsu no wɔ asaase nyinara enyi: na ɔtseen ne nsa suoo no mu, na ɔdze no baa ne nkyɛn wɔ hɛn no mu.

ref: Seven days later Noah sent the dove out again.
text: Na ɔtweɔɔn nda esuon so bio; na ɔsomaa ebubur no fii hɛn no mu bio;

ref: It returned in the evening, holding in its beak a green leaf from an olive tree. Noah knew the water was finally going down.
text: na ebubur no sanee baa no hɔ ewimbir no; na, hwɛ, olive ahataw momon a ɔatsew tua n’ano: nna Noa hun dɛ nsu no atwe efi asaase do.

ref: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.
text: Na ɔtweɔɔn nda esuon so bio; na ɔsomaa ebubur no; na ɔannsan ammba no hɔ bio.

ref: By the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was completely dry.
text: Na bosoom a otsia ebien no mu, bosoom no ne da a otsia eduonu esuon no, nna asaase do wewee.

ref: God said to Noah,
text: Na Nyankopɔn kasaa kyerɛɛ Noa dɛ,

ref: “You, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
text: Fi hɛn no mu pue, ɔwo nye wo yer, nye wo mba, nye wo mba hɔn yernom no ka wo ho.

ref: After Noah and his family had left the boat,
text: Na Noa nye ne yer na ne mba, nye ne mba hɔn yernom ka no ho fiir edzi:

ref: the living creatures left in groups of their own kind. The Lord's Promise for the Earth
text: mbowadoma nketsebowa nyinara, nye ntumbowa nyinara, na adze biara a nkwa wɔ mu wɔ asaase do, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn mbowa ban tsetsee, fii hɛn no mu puei. Noa N’afɔrbɔ

ref: Noah built an altar where he could offer sacrifices to the Lord. Then he offered on the altar one of each kind of animal and bird that could be used for a sacrifice.
text: Na Noa sii afɔrmbukyia maa EWURADZE; na ɔfaa mbowa a hɔn ho tsew nyinara mu bi, na ɔdze hɔn bɔɔ ɔhyew afɔr wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do.

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Na EWURADZE tsee afɔr no no huam; na EWURADZE kaa wɔ n’akoma mu dɛ, Monnkɔdom asaase bio ara da wɔ nyimpa ntsi, osiandɛ minyim dɛ nyimpa n’akoma mu nsusui muo dze fitsi no mbofra mu; nkyii so mennkɛsɛɛ adze a nkwa wɔ mu biara bio ara da, dɛ mbrɛ mayɛ yi.

ref: As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat; winter and summer, day and night.
text: Mber tsentsen ara a asaase wɔ hɔ yi, duaber nye buber, awɔwber nye ɔhyewber, ɔpɛ nye esusow, adzekyee nye adzesaa nnkegyaa da.

ref: God said to Noah and his sons: I am giving you my blessing. Have a lot of children and grandchildren, so people will live everywhere on this earth.
text: Na Nyankopɔn hyiraa Noa nye ne mba, na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Hom nwo, na hom ndɔr, na wɔnyɛ asaase do ma.

ref: All animals, birds, reptiles, and fish will be afraid of you. I have placed them under your control,
text: Na hom ho suro nye hom ho hu mbra asaase do mbowadoma nyinara nye wimu ntumbowa nyinara do; dza wɔwea daadze nyinara, na po mu nam nyinara, wɔdze ahyɛ hom nsa.

ref: and I have given them to you for food. From now on, you may eat them, as well as the green plants that you have always eaten.
text: Adze a nkwa wɔ mu nyinara ɔnyɛ edziban mma hom; dɛ mbrɛ medze nhaban nhaban so ama hom no.

ref: But life is in the blood, and you must not eat any meat that still has blood in it.
text: Na nam a no bɔgyaa da ho wɔ no mu dze, mma hom nndzi.

ref: I want you and your descendants to have many children, so people will live everywhere on earth.
text: Na hom dze, hom nwo menyamenya na hom ndɔr wɔ asaase do.

ref: Again, God said to Noah and his sons:
text: Na Nyankopɔn kasaa kyerɛɛ Noa, nye ne mba a wɔka no ho no dɛ,

ref: I am going to make a solemn promise to you and to everyone who will live after you.
text: Na hwɛ emi, merema m’ahyɛmu etsim ama hom, nye hom asefo a wodzi hom ekyir;

ref: I promise every living creature that the earth and those living on it will never again be destroyed by a flood.
text: Na mebɛma menye hom hɛn ahyɛmu etsim; nkyii so nsuyirii nnkotwa honam nyinara efi hɔ bio; nkyii so nsuyirii nnkɛba bio abɛsɛɛ asaase.

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,
text: Na ɔbɛba dɛ, sɛ medze mununkum meba asaase do a, wobohu nyankontɔn no wɔ mununkum no mu,

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: na mebɛkaa m’ahyɛmu a ɔda emi nye hom nye honam nyinara mu abɔdze a nkwa wɔ mu nyinara ntamu; na nsu no nnkɛyɛ nsuyirii bio asɛɛ honam nyinara.

ref: When I see the rainbow in the sky, I will always remember the promise that I have made to every living creature.
text: Na nyankontɔn no bɛbew mununkum no mu; na mobɔhwɛ, ama maakaa daa ahyɛmu a ɔda Nyankopɔn nye honam nyinara mu abɔdze a nkwa wɔ mu a ɔwɔ asaase do nyinara ntamu.

ref: Noah and his sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, left the boat. Ham later had a son named Canaan.
text: Na Noa ne mba a wofii hɛn mu puei nye Sem, nye Ham, nye Jafet: na Ham nye Keenan n’egya.

ref: All people on earth are descendants of Noah's three sons.
text: Woyinom baasa yi nye Noa ne mba no: na woyinom mu na nyimpa fi petsee asaase nyinara do.

ref: Noah farmed the land and was the first to plant a vineyard.
text: Na Noa hyɛɛ ase yɛɛ kua, na ɔyɛɛ wenyin ture:

ref: One day he got drunk and was lying naked in his tent.
text: na ɔnoom wenyin no bi, na ɔbowee; na no ho daa hɔ wɔ n’asesee mu.

ref: Ham entered the tent and saw him naked, then went back outside and told his brothers.
text: Na Keenan n’egya Ham hun n’egya n’adagyaw, na ɔkɛkaa kyerɛɛ no nuanom beenu no wɔ abowano.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Na Sem nye Jafet faa tam, dze guu hɔn batsir, na wɔnatsewee dze no hɔn ekyir hɔn ekyir dze kɛkataa hɔn egya n’adagyaw do; nna hɔn enyim wɔ ekyir, na woennhu hɔn egya n’adagyaw.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Na Noa no wenyin no fii n’enyi do, na ɔtsee dza ne ba kuma no dze ayɛ no.

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: Nna ɔsee dɛ, Ndom nka Keenan; Nkowaa mu akowaa na ɔbɛyɛ ama no nuanom.

ref: I ask the Lord my God to bless Shem and make Canaan his slave.
text: Na ɔsee bio dɛ, Nhyira nka EWURADZE, Sem ne Nyankopɔn; Na wɔmma Keenan nyɛ n’akowaa.

ref: I pray God will give Japheth more and more land and let him take over the territory of Shem. May Canaan be his slave.”
text: Nyankopɔn nyɛ Jafet kɛse, Na wɔmma ɔntsena Sem n’asesee mu; Na wɔmma Keenan nyɛ n’akowaa.

ref: and died at the age of
text: na Noa ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron eduonum: na owui.

ref: After the flood Shem, Ham, and Japheth had many descendants. The Descendants of Japheth
text: Afei iyinom nye Noa ne mba, Sem, Ham nye Jafet hɔn ebusuasantsen a nsuyirii no n’ekyir no wɔwoo hɔn.

ref: Japheth's descendants had their own languages, tribes, and land. They were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras. Gomer was the ancestor of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah. Javan was the ancestor of Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim, who settled along the coast. The Descendants of Ham
text: Woyinom hɔn mu na nsuano amanaman fi petsee hɔn asaase do, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn nyinara hɔn kasaa tse; dɛ mbrɛ hɔn ebusua ebusua tsetsee, wɔ hɔn amanaman mu.

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Woyinom nye Noa ne mba hɔn ebusuasantsen; na woyinom hɔn mu na nsuyirii no n’ekyir no amanaman fi petsee asaase do.

ref: At first everyone spoke the same language,
text: Nna asaase nyinara hɔn kasaa kor.

ref: but after some of them moved from the east and settled in Babylonia,
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, wotuu kwan rokɔ boka no, wohun asaase tɛtɛr bi wɔ Sinar asaase do; na wɔtsenaa hɔ.

ref: they said: Let's build a city with a tower that reaches to the sky! We'll use hard bricks and tar instead of stone and mortar. We'll become famous, and we won't be scattered all over the world.
text: Na wɔsee dɛ, Wɔmbra, wɔmma yɛnkyekyer kurow, na yɛntow abantsentsen a n’etsifi bodu sor, na wɔmma yɛmfa ngye dzin; amma yaammpetse asaase nyinara enyi.

ref: But when the Lord came down to look at the city and the tower,
text: Na EWURADZE sianee bɔhwɛɛ kurow no nye abantsentsen no a adasamba rotow no.

ref: he said: These people are working together because they all speak the same language. This is just the beginning. Soon they will be able to do anything they want.
text: Na EWURADZE see dɛ, Hwɛ, wɔyɛ ɔman kor, na hɔn nyinara hɔn kasaa kor; na dza wɔahyɛ ase reyɛ nye yi: na afei biribiara a wobosusu dɛ wɔbɛyɛ no, bibiribiara nnkotum esiw hɔn kwan.

ref: Let's go down and confuse them! We'll make them speak different languages, and they won't be able to understand each other.
text: Wɔmbra, wɔmma yensian, na yɛnkɔtoto hɔn kasaa wɔ hɔ, amma obi aanntse ne nyɛnko ne nsɛm ase.

ref: So the people had to stop building the city, because the Lord confused their language and scattered them all over the earth. That's how the city of Babel got its name. The Descendants of Shem
text: Dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Babel; osiandɛ hɔ na EWURADZE totoo asaase nyinara hɔn kasaa: na hɔ na EWURADZE fi bɔɔ hɔn petsee asaase nyinara enyi. Sem Asefo

ref: Two years after the flood, when Shem was
text: Sem woo Arpaksad n’ekyir no odzii mfe ahaenu, na ɔwoo mbabanyin nye mbabaa.

ref: Arpachshad had more children and died at the age of
text: na Arpaksad woo Sela no n’ekyir odzii mfe ahaanan ebiasa na ɔwoo mbabanyin nye mbabaa.

ref: Shelah had more children and died at the age of
text: na Sela woo Eber n’ekyir odzii mfe ahaanan ebiasa, na ɔwoo mbabanyin nye mbabaa.

ref: Eber had more children and died at the age of
text: na Eber woo Peleg no n’ekyir odzii mfe ahaanan eduasa, na ɔwoo mbabanyin nye mbabaa.

ref: Peleg had more children and died at the age of
text: na ɔwoo Reu n’ekyir no odzii mfe ahaenu nye akron, na ɔwoo mbabanyin nye mbabaa.

ref: Reu had more children and died at the age of
text: na Serug n’ekyir no odzii mfe ahaenu nye esuon, na ɔwoo mbabanyin nye mbabaa.

ref: Serug had more children and died at the age of
text: na Nahor n’ekyir no odzii mfe ahaenu, na ɔwoo mbabanyin nye mbabaa.

ref: Nahor had more children and died at the age of
text: na ɔwoo Tera no n’ekyir odzii mfe ɔha duakron, na ɔwoo mbabanyin nye mbabaa.

ref: Terah decided to move from Ur to the land of Canaan. He took along Abram and Sarai and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran. But when they came to the city of Haran, they settled there instead.
text: Na Tera faa ne ba Abram, ne nana Lot a ɔyɛ Haran ne ba no nye Abram ne yer Sarai na ɔnye hɔn sii mu fi Kaldeafo Ur, dɛ wɔrokɔ Keenan asaase do; na wɔbaa Haran bɛtsenaa hɔ.

ref: The Lord said to Abram: Leave your country, your family, and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.
text: Afei EWURADZE see Abram dɛ, Fi wo man mu, na fi w’ebusua mu, na fi w’egya fie, kɔ asaase a mebɛkyerɛ wo no do:

ref: I will bless you and make your descendants into a great nation. You will become famous and be a blessing to others.
text: na mebɛyɛ wo ɔman kɛse, na mibehyira wo, na mama wo dzin ayɛ kɛse: na ayɛ nhyira:

ref: I will bless those who bless you, but I will put a curse on anyone who puts a curse on you. Everyone on earth will be blessed because of you.
text: na mibehyira hɔn a wohyira wo, na nyia ɔdom wo no mobɔdom no: na wo mu na wobehyira asaase ebusua ebusua nyinara.

ref: Abram went as far as the sacred tree of Moreh in a place called Shechem. The Canaanites were still living in the land at that time,
text: Na Abram twaa mu wɔ asaase no do kɔr beebi a wɔfrɛ hɔ Sekem, kɔr More esudur no ho. Na aber no nna Keenanfo wɔ asaase no do.

ref: but the Lord appeared to Abram and promised, “I will give this land to your family forever.” Abram then built an altar there for the Lord.
text: Na EWURADZE yii noho kyerɛɛ Abram, na ɔsee dɛ, W’asefo na medze asaase yi bɛma hɔn: na hɔ na Abram sii afɔrmbukyia maa EWURADZE a oyii noho kyerɛɛ no no.

ref: Abram traveled to the hill country east of Bethel and camped between Bethel and Ai, where he built another altar and worshiped the Lord.
text: Na ofii hɔ kɔr bepɔw a ɔwɔ Bɛtɛl no boka no do, na hɔ na ɔbɔɔ n’asesee, a Bɛtɛl dzi n’anee, na Ai dzi no boka: na osii afɔrmbukyia maa EWURADZE wɔ hɔ na ɔsɔɔr EWURADZE dzin.

ref: Later, Abram started out toward the Southern Desert. Abram in Egypt
text: Na Abram tuu kwan, dɛ ɔda ho ara rokɔ Pomu afamu. Abram Wɔ Egypt

ref: The crops failed, and there was no food anywhere in Canaan. So Abram and his wife Sarai went to live in Egypt for a while. But just before they got there, Abram said, “Sarai, you are really beautiful!
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aka kakra ma ɔahɛn Egypt no, ɔsee ne yer Sarai dɛ, Minyim dɛ eyɛ ɔbaa a wo ho yɛ fɛw:

ref: When the Egyptians see you, they will murder me because I am your husband. But they won't kill you.
text: na ɔbɛba dɛ sɛ Egyptfo no hu wo a, wɔbɛse dɛ, Ne yer nye yi: na woboku emi, na wɔagye ɔwo nkwa.

ref: As soon as Abram and Sarai arrived in Egypt, the Egyptians noticed how beautiful she was.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a Abram baa Egypt no, Egyptfo no hun ɔbaa no dɛ no ho yɛ fɛw papa.

ref: The king's officials told him about her, and she was taken to his house.
text: Na Farao ne mbapɔmba no hun no, na wɔbɛkamfoo no kyerɛɛ Farao: na wɔbɛfaa ɔbaa no kɔr Farao fie.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Na ɔyɛɛ Abram yie wɔ ne ntsi: na onyaa nguan, nye anantwi, nye nsoasopɔnkɔnyin, nye nkowaa, nye mfonabaa, nye nsoasopɔnkɔber, nye efumpɔnkɔ.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Na osian Abram yer Sarai ntsi EWURADZE dze ɔyardɔm akɛse haaw Farao nye ne fi.

ref: Finally, the king sent for Abram and said to him, “What have you done to me? Why didn't you tell me Sarai was your wife?
text: Na Farao frɛɛ Abram, na ɔsee dɛ, Ebɛnadze na edze ayɛ me yi? Ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na annka annkyerɛ me dɛ wo yer a?

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na esee dɛ, M’akyerɛbaa a ma dɛm ntsi mefaa no nkyɛ morowar no yi? Dɛm ntsi afei, hwɛ, wo yer nye no, fa no, na kɔ wo kwan.

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Na Farao hyɛɛ nkorɔfo ma wokegyaa ɔno, nye ne yer, nye dza ɔwɔ nyinara wɔ ɔkwan do.

ref: Abram and Sarai took everything they owned and went to the Southern Desert. Lot went with them.
text: Na Abram fii Egypt, ɔno, nye ne yer, nye dza ɔwɔ nyinara, nye Lot ka no ho, kɔr Pomu afamu.

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Na Abram nyaa no ho kɛsenara wɔ fiembowa, nye dwetɛ, nye sika mu.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Na otuu no kwan fi Pomu afamu araa kodur Bɛtɛl, beebi mbrɛ ahyɛse no n’asesee wɔ mu no, wɔ Bɛtɛl nye Ai ntamu:

ref: where he had earlier camped and built an altar. There he worshiped the Lord.
text: beebi mbrɛ afɔrmbukyia a asɔpɔn no ɔyɛɛ no wɔ hɔ no wɔ no: na hɔ na Abram sɔɔr EWURADZE.

ref: Lot, who was traveling with him, also had sheep, goats, and cattle, as well as his own family and slaves.
text: Nna Lot a ɔnye Abram nam no so wɔ nguan, nye anantwi, nye asese.

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Na Abram see Lot dɛ, Meserɛ wo, mma emma aperper mmba emi nye ɔwo ntamu, na mo mbowahwɛfo nye wo mbowahwɛfo ntamu; osiandɛ yɛyɛ enuanom.

ref: There is plenty of land for you to choose from. Let's separate. If you go north, I'll go south; if you go south, I'll go north.”
text: Ana nnyɛ asaase no nyinara na ɔda w’enyim yi bi? Meserɛ wo, tsew woho fi mo ho: sɛ bankum na ebɛfa a, ɔno mo so mobɔkɔ nyimfa; anaa sɛ nyimfa na ebɛfa a, ɔno mo so mobɔkɔ bankum.

ref: This happened before the Lord had destroyed the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. And when Lot looked around, he saw there was plenty of water in the Jordan Valley. All the way to Zoar the valley was as green as the garden of the Lord or the land of Egypt.
text: Na Lot maa n’enyi do, na ɔhwɛɛ Jordan Tɛtɛrmu nyinara dze rokɔ Zoar, na ohun dɛ no do beebiara wɔ nsu papaapa. Iyi ekyir ansaana EWURADZE resɛɛ Sodom nye Gomora, tse dɛ EWURADZE no ture, nna ɔtse dɛ Egypt asaase.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: Nna Lot faa Jordan Tɛtɛrmu nyinara; na Lot tui faa boka afamu: na ɔbaako paa noho fii ne nyɛnko ho.

ref: Abram stayed in the land of Canaan. But Lot settled near the cities of the valley and put up his tents not far from Sodom,
text: Abram tsenaa Keenan asaase do, na Lot tsenaa Tɛtɛrmu kurow no mu, na ɔdze n’asesee tutui araa dze kodur Sodom.

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: Afei Sodomfo yɛ ehuhufo nye ndzebɔnyɛfo kɛsenara tsia EWURADZE.

ref: After Abram and Lot had gone their separate ways, the Lord said to Abram: Look around to the north, south, east, and west.
text: Na Lot paa noho wɔ Abram ho n’ekyir no, EWURADZE see Abram dɛ, Afei ma w’enyi do, na fi beebi mbrɛ ewɔ no hwɛ kwaamu afamu nye pomu afamu nye boka afamu nye anee afamu:

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: na asaase a ihu no nyinara, medze bɛma ɔwo, nye w’asefo daa.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: Na mebɛma w’asefo ayɛ dɛ asaase do huntuma: na sɛ obi tum kan asaase do huntuma a, ɔnodze nna wobotum akan w’asefo no.

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Soɛr, fa asaase no do wɔ ne tsentsen nye tɛtɛr mu; na ɔwo na medze bɛma wo.

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Na Abram tutuu n’asesee, na ɔbae bɛtsenaa Mamre nsudur a ɔwɔ Hebron no ne nkyɛn, na osii afɔrmbukyia wɔ hɔ maa EWURADZE.

ref: About this time, King Amraphel of Babylonia, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ Sinarhen Amrafel, Elasarhen Ariok, Elamhen Kedorlaomer, nye Goiimhen Tidal aber do no,

ref: attacked King Bera of Sodom, King Birsha of Gomorrah, King Shinab of Admah, King Shemeber of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, a city also known as Zoar.
text: wɔnye Sodomhen Bora, nye Gomorahen Birsa, Admahhen Sinab, nye Zeboimfo hen Semeber, nye Belahen yɛɛ ɔsa.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Na afe a otsia duanan no mu na Kedorlaomer na ahemfo a wɔka no ho no bae, na wobokum Refafo wɔ Asterot-karnaim, nye Zuzfo wɔ Ham, nye Emfo wɔ Savɛ-kiriataim,

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.
text: na Horfo wɔ hɔn bepɔw Seir do, dze kodu El-paran a ɔwɔ sar no ho no.

ref: They went back to the city of Enmishpat, better known as Kadesh. Then they captured all the land that belonged to the Amalekites, and they defeated the Amorites who were living in Hazazon-Tamar.
text: Na wɔsanee, na wɔbaa En-mispat , na wɔbobɔɔ Amalekfo man nyinara, nye Amorifo so a wɔtse Hazazon-tamar no.

ref: was full of tar pits, and when the troops from Sodom and Gomorrah started running away, some of them fell into the pits. Others escaped to the hill country.
text: Nna hua amona wɔ Siddim bɔnsa mu ma ara tɔ; na ber Sodom nye Gomora ahemfo no ruguan no, binom koguu mu na hɔn a wɔkae no guanee kɔr bepɔw no do.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Na wɔfaa Sodom nye Gomora ndzɛmba nyinara, na hɔn edzidzidze nyinara, na wɔkɔr hɔn kwan.

ref: They also captured Abram's nephew Lot, who lived in Sodom. They took him and his possessions and then left.
text: Na wɔfaa Lot, Abram no nua ba a ɔtse Sodom no, nye ne ndzɛmba, na wosii mu kɔree.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: Na obi a ofii nsa bae bɛkaa kyerɛɛ Hebriwnyi Abram a aber yi nna ɔtse Amorinyi Mamre, Eskol nye Aner hɔn nua no no nsudur ho no; nna woyinom ɔnye Abram akyekyer.

ref: that his nephew Lot had been taken away. Three hundred and eighteen of Abram's servants were fighting men, so he took them and followed the enemy as far north as the city of Dan.
text: Na aber a Abram tsee dɛ wɔafa no nua ne ba ndɔmmum no, ɔdze no nkorɔfo a wɔatsetse hɔn a wɔwoo hɔn wɔ ne fie, ahaasa duawɔtwe tui, na wotsiiw hɔn araa kodur Dan.

ref: That night, Abram divided up his troops, attacked from all sides, and won a great victory. But some of the enemy escaped to the town of Hobah north of Damascus,
text: Na ɔno nye no nkowaa kyɛɛ hɔnho mu kɔr hɔn do anafua, na wɔbɔɔ hɔn gui, na wotsiiw hɔn kodur Hoba a ɔwɔ Damaskɔs n’abankum no.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: Na ɔdze ndzɛmba no nyinara sanee bae, ɔdze no nua ne ba Lot, nye ne ndzɛmba, nye mbaa no so, nye nkorɔfo no, sanee bae. Melkizedek Hyira Abram

ref: Abram returned after he had defeated King Chedorlaomer and the other kings. Then the king of Sodom went to meet Abram in Shaveh Valley, which is also known as King's Valley.
text: Na ɔsanee fi Kedorlaomer nye ahemfo a wɔka no ho no hɔn do nkonyimdzi no reba no, Sodomhen puei kehyiaa no kwan wɔ Savɛ bɔnsa mu .

ref: King Melchizedek of Salem was a priest of God Most High. He brought out some bread and wine
text: Na Salemhen Melkizedek dze paanoo nye wenyin bae: nna ɔyɛ Abɔso Nyankopɔn no sɔfoe.

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Na ohyiaa no, na ɔsee dɛ, Nhyira mfi Abɔso Nyankopɔn a ɔsor nye asaase yɛ nedze no hɔ nka Abram;

ref: All praise belongs to God Most High for helping you defeat your enemies.” Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything.
text: na nhyira nka Abɔso Nyankopɔn a ɔdze w’atamfo ahyɛ wo nsa. Na Abram maa no ndzɛmba no nyinara mu ebupɛn du du.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Na Sodomhen see Abram dɛ, Fa nyimpa no ma me, na woara fa ndzɛmba no.

ref: Abram answered: The Lord God Most High made the heavens and the earth. And I have promised him
text: Na Abram see Sodomhen no dɛ, Mama me nsa do makyerɛ EWURADZE, Abɔso Nyankopɔn a ɔsor nye asaase yɛ nedze no,

ref: that I won't keep anything of yours, not even a sandal strap or a piece of thread. Then you can never say that you are the one who made me rich.
text: dɛ mennkɛfa ahoma anaa mpaboaahoma anaa biribiara a ɔyɛ wodze, amma aannka dɛ, Mayɛ Abram birɛmpɔn:

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: gyedɛ dza mberantsɛ no edzi no nko, na Aner, Eskol nye Mamre a wɔnye me kɔree no hɔn kyɛpɛn.

ref: Later the Lord spoke to Abram in a vision, “Abram, don't be afraid! I will protect you and reward you greatly.”
text: Iyinom ekyir no EWURADZE n’asɛm baa Abram hɔ wɔ enyido adzehu mu dɛ, Mma nnsuro, Abram: Emi nye wo kokyɛm, na mebɛma wo akatua a ɔbor do.

ref: But Abram answered, “Lord All-Powerful, you have given me everything I could ask for, except children. And when I die, Eliezer of Damascus will get all I own.
text: Na Abram see dɛ, EWURADZE Nyankopɔn, ebɛnadze na edze bɛma me, hwɛ dɛ minnyi ba na menam, na nyia obedzi m’adze nye Eliezer a ofi Damaskɔs anaa?

ref: You have not given me any children, and this servant of mine will inherit everything.”
text: Na Abram see dɛ, Hwɛ, emi dze, emmaa me asefo: na, hwɛ, nyia wɔawo no wɔ me fie na obedzi m’adze.

ref: The Lord replied, “No, he won't! You will have a son of your own, and everything you have will be his.”
text: Na, hwɛ, EWURADZE n’asɛm baa no hɔ dɛ, Nnyɛ oyi bi na ɔbɛyɛ wo dziadzenyi; na dza ofi woara wo nson mu bɛba no, ɔno na obedzi w’adze.

ref: Then the Lord took Abram outside and said, “Look at the sky and see if you can count the stars. That's how many descendants you will have.”
text: Na ɔdze no puei, na ɔsee dɛ, Afei hwɛ sor, na kan nworaba no, kyerɛ dɛ ibotum akan a: na ɔsee no dɛ, Mbrɛ w’asefo bɛyɛ nye no.

ref: Abram believed the Lord, so the Lord was pleased with him and accepted him. The Lord Makes Another Promise to Abram
text: Na ɔgyee EWURADZE dzii; na EWURADZE buu ne gyedzi ndzeyɛtsenenee maa no.

ref: The Lord said to Abram, “I brought you here from Ur in Chaldea, and I gave you this land.”
text: Na ɔsee no dɛ, Emi nye EWURADZE a medze wo fi Kaldeafo Ur wɔ Kaldea bae, dɛ medze asaase yi rebɛma wo, ma edze aayɛ egyapadze.

ref: Abram asked, “Lord God, how can I know the land will be mine?”
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, EWURADZE Nyankopɔn, medze ebɛnadze na medze bohu dɛ ɔbɛyɛ m’egyapadze?

ref: Then the Lord told him, “Bring me a three-year-old cow, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.”
text: Na ɔsee no dɛ, Fa nantwiba a oedzi mfe ebiasa, nye apɔnkyeber a oedzi mfe ebiasa, nye oguannyin a oedzi mfe ebiasa, nye atamram, nye aborɔnomaba brɛ me.

ref: Abram obeyed the Lord. Then he cut the animals in half and laid the two halves of each animal opposite each other on the ground. But he did not cut the doves and pigeons in half.
text: Na ɔdze iyinom nyinara bae, na ɔpaapaa hɔn mu ebien ebien, na ɔdze afa afa no totoo hɔn afa afa ne pɛn do: na nnoma no dze ɔammpaapaa hɔn mu.

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Na nnoma a wodzi nam bɛgyegyee mbowa fun no do, na Abram kaa hɔn.

ref: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and everything became dark and frightening.
text: Na aber a wi rokɔtɔ no, nda heii tɔɔ Abram do; na, hwɛ, sum kɛsenara na hu tɔɔ no do.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: Na mbasantsen a otsia anan no mu na wɔbɛba ha bio: osiandɛ Amorifo emumuyɛ nnyae nnyɛɛ ma.

ref: Sometime after sunset, when it was very dark, a smoking cooking pot and a flaming fire passed between the two halves of each animal.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a iwi kɔtɔree, na ɔyɛɛ sum no, hwɛ, fononoo a orupun wusiw, nye ɔtsɛn a ɔredzɛw botwaa mu wɔ afa afa no ntamu.

ref: At that time the Lord made an agreement with Abram and told him: I will give your descendants the land east of the Shihor River on the border of Egypt as far as the Euphrates River.
text: Da no ara mu na EWURADZE nye Abram dzii ahyɛmu see dɛ, W’asefo na medze asaase yi ama hɔn, ofi Egypt esutsen no ho kodu esutsen kɛse no, Eufrates esutsen no ho:

ref: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaites,
text: Hetfo, Perizfo, Refafo,

ref: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
text: Amorifo, Keenanfo, Girgasfo, nye Jebusfo asaase no.

ref: Abram's wife Sarai had not been able to have any children. But she owned a young Egyptian slave woman named Hagar,
text: Afei Abram ne yer Sarai ɔnnwo mma no: mbom nna ɔwɔ afonabaa Egyptnyi bi a ne dzin dze Hagar.

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: Na Sarai see Abram dɛ, Afei hwɛ, EWURADZE esiw me awoo kwan; meserɛ wo, kɔ m’afonabaa nkyɛn; annyɛ a mibenya no ho mba. Na Abram tsiee Sarai ne ndze.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.
text: Na Abram tsenaa Keenan asaase do mfe du n’ekyir no, Abram ne yer Sarai faa n’afonabaa Hagar, Egyptnyi no, na ɔdze no maa no kun Abram awar.

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Na ɔkɔr Hagar nkyɛn, na onyinsenii: na aber a ohun dɛ onyinsen no, obuu no wuraba enyimtsia.

ref: Then Sarai said to Abram, “It's all your fault! I gave you my slave woman, but she has been hateful to me ever since she found out she was pregnant. You have done me wrong, and you will have to answer to the Lord for this.”
text: Na Sarai see Abram dɛ, Mo mfom da wo do; medze m’afonabaa too wo bo mu, na ohun dɛ onyinsen ntsi, obu me enyimtsia: EWURADZE mbua emi nye ɔwo ntamu atsɛn.

ref: Abram said, “All right! She's your slave—do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai began treating Hagar so harshly that she finally ran away.
text: Na Abram see Sarai dɛ, Hwɛ, w’afonabaa wɔ wo nsamu, yɛ no dɛ mbrɛ woara ehwɛ a oye no ara. Na Sarai nye no dzii no dzendzenndzen, ma ofii n’enyim guanee.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Na EWURADZE no bɔfoe hun no wɔ nsunyiwa bi ho wɔ sar do, Sur kwan mu nsunyiwa no ho a.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: Na obisaa no dɛ, Sarai n’afonabaa Hagar, ifi henfa bae na erokɔ henfa? Na obuaa dɛ, Mifi mo wuraba Sarai n’enyim na muruguan.

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.
text: Na EWURADZE no bɔfoe no see no dɛ, San kɔ wo wuraba hɔ, na kɛbrɛ woho adze wɔ n’ase.

ref: I will give you a son, who will be called Ishmael, because I have heard your cry for help. And someday I will give you so many descendants that no one will be able to count them all.
text: Na EWURADZE no bɔfoe no see no dɛ, Hwɛ, enyinsen, na ebɔwo babanyin; na ebɛfrɛ ne dzin Ismael, osiandɛ EWURADZE atse w’amandzehu.

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Na ɔbɛyɛ dɛ asoaso bɔyafo wɔ adasa mu; ne nsa betsia nyimpa nyinara, na nyimpa nyinara nsa etsia no; na ɔbɔsoɛr etsia no nuanom nyinara.

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Na ɔfrɛɛ EWURADZE a ɔkasaa kyerɛɛ no no ne dzin dɛ, ɔwo yɛ Nyankopɔn a ihu adze: na ɔkaa dɛ, Aso ha ara mpo mehu nyia ohu me yi?

ref: That's why people call the well between Kadesh and Bered, “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ bura no Beer-lahai-roi; na, ɔwɔ Kades nye Bered ntamu.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Na mebɛma m’ahyɛmu ada emi nye ɔwo ntamu, na mebɛma adɔr mbordo.

ref: Abram bowed with his face to the ground, and God said:
text: Na Abram dze n’enyim boom famu: na Nyankopɔn nye no kasae see dɛ,

ref: I promise that you will be the father of many nations. So now I'm changing your name from Abram to Abraham.
text: Nkyii so wɔnnkɛfrɛ wo dzin Abram bio, na wɔbɛfrɛ wo dzin Abraham; osiandɛ amanaman pii hɔn egya na mayɛ wo.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: Na mebɛma awo mbordo, na mayɛ wo amanaman, na ahemfo efi wo mu epue.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: Na mebɛma m’ahyɛmu etsim wɔ emi nye ɔwo nye w’asefo a wodzi w’ekyir no ntamu wɔ hɔn mbasantsen mu, na ɔayɛ daa ahyɛmu, dɛ mebɛyɛ Nyankopɔn ama ɔwo nye w’asefo a wodzi w’ekyir no.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na medze asaase a etsenatsena do, yɛ do hɔho yi, Keenan asaase nyinara, mebɛma ɔwo nye w’asefo a wodzi w’ekyir, dɛ daa enyadze; na mebɛyɛ hɔn Nyankopɔn.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see Abraham dɛ, ɔwo dze, sie m’ahyɛmu, ɔwo nye w’asefo a wodzi w’ekyir, wɔ hɔn mbasantsen mu.

ref: As the sign that you are keeping this promise, you must circumcise every man and boy in your family.
text: Na wontwitwa hom ho twatsia; na ɔbɛyɛ ahyɛmu a ɔda emi nye hom ntamu asɛnkyerɛdze.

ref: Any man who isn't circumcised hasn't kept his part of the promise and cannot be one of my people.
text: Na ɔbanyin a wonntwaa no twatsia no, dɛm nyimpa no wontwa no mfi no nkorɔfo mu; ɔato m’ahyɛmu.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see Abraham dɛ, Wo yer Sarai dze, mma mmfrɛ ne dzin Sarai, na Sara nyɛ ne dzin.

ref: I will bless her, and you will have a son by her. She will become the mother of nations, and some of her descendants will even be kings.
text: Na mibehyira no, ekyir yi so mebɛma enye no awo babanyin: nyew, mibehyira no, na ɔbɛyɛ amanaman hɔn na; aman ahemfo befi no mu epue.

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Na Abraham see Nyankopɔn dɛ, kɛtseankyɛ Ismael eenya adom:

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Ampaara ampa, wo yer Sara bɔwo babanyin ama wo; na ebɛfrɛ ne dzin Isaak: na menye no bedzi ahyɛmu ma aayɛ daa ahyɛmu dze ama n’asefo a wodzi n’ekyir.

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: Na Ismael ho dze metsie wo; hwɛ, mehyira no, na mebɛma ɔawo, na ɔadɔr mbordo; ɔbɔwo etsitsir duebien, na mebɛyɛ no ɔman kɛse.

ref: But your son Isaac will be born about this time next year, and the promise I am making to you and your family will be for him and his descendants forever.
text: Na m’ahyɛmu dze mebɛma oetsim wɔ emi nye Isaak a Sara bɔwo no ama wo afedan sisei ntamu.

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Na ɔnye no gyaa kasa no, Nyankopɔn fii Abraham nkyɛn kɔr sor.

ref: On that same day Abraham obeyed God by circumcising Ishmael. Abraham was also circumcised, and so were all the other men and boys in his household, including his servants and slaves. He was
text: Na ne fie mbanyin nyinara, hɔn a wɔwoo hɔn wɔ fie, nye hɔn a wɔdze sika totɔɔ hɔn ahɔho hɔ, wɔka no ho ma wotwitwaa hɔn twatsia.

ref: One hot summer afternoon while Abraham was sitting by the entrance to his tent near the sacred trees of Mamre, the Lord appeared to him.
text: Na EWURADZE yii noho kyerɛɛ Abram wɔ Mamre nsudur nkyɛn, ewia ketee a ɔtse n’asesee abowano no;

ref: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. He quickly ran to meet them, bowed with his face to the ground,
text: na ɔmaa n’enyi do, na ɔhwɛe, na, hwɛ, mbarimba baasa gyina ne tsentsendo. Na aber a ohun hɔn no, otutuu mbirika fii n’asesee abowano kehyiaa hɔn kwan, na ɔkotoow famu,

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: na ɔsee dɛ, Mo wura, sɛ afei menya w’enyim enyimnyam a, meserɛ wo, mma mmpa w’akowaa ho nnkɔ.

ref: I'll have some water brought, so you can wash your feet, then you can rest under a tree.
text: Afei wɔmma wɔnkɛfa nsu kakra mbra, na hom nhohor hom anan ho, na hom ngye hom ahom wɔ dua yi ase:

ref: Let me get you some food to give you strength before you leave. I would be honored to serve you.” “Thank you very much,” they answered. “We accept your offer.”
text: na monkɔ nkɛfa edziban trɔma bi mbra ma hom mfa nhyɛ hom akoma dzen; na n’ekyir no hom akɔ: susuampa dɛ hom aba hom akowaa nkyɛn yi. Na wɔsee dɛ, Yɛ dɛ mbrɛ aka no ara.

ref: Abraham went quickly to his tent and said to Sarah, “Hurry! Get a large sack of flour and make some bread.”
text: Na Abraham kaa noho kɔr asese no mu Sara hɔ, na ɔsee dɛ, Yɛ isiam pa etwadze ebiasa krado ntsɛntsɛm, fetsew, na to tatar.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Na Abraham tutuu enguan kɔr no mbowakuw mu, na ɔkɛfaa nantwiba a ɔyɛ mberɛw na oye, na ɔdze maa ɔsomfo; na ɔyɛɛ ntsɛm kɛyɛɛ edziban.

ref: He then served his guests some yogurt and milk together with the meat. While they were eating, he stood near them under the tree,
text: Na Abraham faa bata, nye merekye, nye nantwiba a ɔdze ayɛ edziban no, na ɔdze besii hɔn enyim: na ogyinaa hɔn nkyɛn wɔ dua no ase, na wodzidzii.

ref: and they asked, “Where's your wife Sarah?” “She is right there in the tent,” Abraham answered.
text: Na wobisaa no dɛ, Wo yer Sara wɔ hen? Na ɔsee dɛ, Hwɛ, ɔwɔ asese no mu.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Afei nna Abraham nye Sara enyinyin, na woedzi mfe ara yie; na adze a ɔyɛ mbaa no, etwa wɔ Sara ho.

ref: So she laughed and said to herself, “Now that I am worn out and my husband is old, will I really know such happiness?”
text: Na Sara sereew noara no mu see dɛ, Aso mayɛ aberwa yi n’ekyir na mibenya enyigye, mo wura so ayɛ akɔdaa yi a?

ref: The Lord asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh? Does she doubt that she can have a child in her old age?
text: Na EWURADZE bisaa Abraham dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na Sara serewee see dɛ, Aso ampaara dɛ mayɛ aberwa yi mobɔwo ba?

ref: I am the Lord! There is nothing too difficult for me. I'll come back next year at the time I promised, and Sarah will already have a son.”
text: Ana biribi yɛ dzen ma EWURADZE a? Aber a mahyɛ no mebɛsan maba wo nkyɛn afedan sisei, na Sara benya babanyin.

ref: Sarah was so frightened that she lied and said, “I didn't laugh.” “Yes, you did!” he answered. Abraham Prays for Sodom
text: Na Sara sanee see dɛ, Mannserew bi; osiandɛ osuroi. Na ɔsee dɛ, Dabida, na eserewee. Abraham Dzi Ma Sodom

ref: When the three men got ready to leave, they looked down toward Sodom, and Abraham walked part of the way with them.
text: Na mbarimba no soɛree fii hɔ, na wɔdaan hɔn enyi kɔr Sodom do: na Abraham kegyaa hɔn kwan.

ref: The Lord said to himself, “I should tell Abraham what I am going to do,
text: Na EWURADZE see dɛ, Ana medze dza merebɛyɛ no bosuma Abraham?

ref: since his family will become a great and powerful nation that will be a blessing to all the other nations on earth.
text: Hwɛ, ampaara Abraham bɛyɛ ɔman a ɔso na ɔwɔ tum, na no mu na wobehyira asaase amanaman nyinara.

ref: I have chosen him to teach his family to obey me forever and to do what is right and fair. If they do, I will give Abraham many descendants, just as I promised.”
text: Na masan mu eyi no, ama ɔaahyɛ ne mba nye ne fifo a wodzi n’ekyir, ma woeesie EWURADZE no kwan, wɔayɛ pɛrpɛr nye atsɛmbu; ama EWURADZE dze dza ɔaka no wɔ Abraham ho no aaba mu.

ref: ?” “Because of them, I won't destroy the city,” was the Lord's answer.
text: Na EWURADZE see dɛ, ɔnam dɛ Sodom nye Gomora ho nkekamu ayɛ kɛse; na hɔn bɔn ayɛ dur dodow ntsi;

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: afei mibesian akɔhwɛ kyerɛ dɛ ɔayɛ dɛ mbrɛ nkekamu a edu me nkyɛn no tse no ewie; na sɛ ɔnntse dɛm so a, mubohu.

ref: The men turned and started toward Sodom. But the Lord stayed with Abraham.
text: Na mbarimba beenu no daan hɔnho fii hɔ dɛ wɔrokɔ Sodom: na Abraham dze, ɔdaa ho ara gyinaa EWURADZE n’enyim.

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Na Abraham twee pinii, na obisaa dɛ, Ana edze atseneneefo nye ndzebɔnyɛfo nyinara bɔbɔ mu ahyew hɔn?

ref: You surely wouldn't let them be killed when you destroy the evil ones. You are the judge of all the earth, and you do what is right.”
text: Iyi mpar wo dɛ ebɛyɛ no dɛm, dɛ edze atseneneefo bɛka ndzebɔnyɛfo ho eku hɔn, ma atseneneefo ayɛ dɛ ndzebɔnyɛfo; iyi mpar wo: ana asaase nyinara ɔtsɛmbuafo nnkɛyɛ dza ɔtsen bi a?

ref: Abraham answered, “I am nothing more than the dust of the earth. Please forgive me, Lord, for daring to speak to you like this.
text: Na Abraham buae na ɔsee dɛ, Afei hwɛ, emi a meyɛ dɛtse nye nso, na mesi mo bo dɛ mebɛkasa akyerɛ EWURADZE yi:

ref: Then Abraham said, “I don't have any right to ask you, Lord, but what would you do if you find only
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Afei hwɛ, mesi mo bo dɛ mebɛkasa akyerɛ EWURADZE: egyangyanbiara eduonu na wobohu hɔn wɔ hɔ ɛ: Na ɔsee dɛ, Osian eduonu no ntsi mennkɛsɛɛ no.

ref: Finally, Abraham said, “Please don't get angry, Lord, if I speak just once more. Suppose you find only
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Ao mma mma EWURADZE no bo mmfuw, na menkasa prɛko pɛ yi ara: egyangyanbiara du na wobohu hɔn wɔ hɔ ɛ; Na ɔsee dɛ, Osian du no ntsi mennkɛsɛɛ no.

ref: After speaking with Abraham, the Lord left, and Abraham went back home.
text: Na EWURADZE wiee dɛ ɔnye Abraham kasa ara na ɔkɔr no kwan: na Abraham sanee baa ne tsenabea.

ref: That evening, while Lot was sitting near the city gate, the two angels arrived in Sodom. When Lot saw them, he got up, bowed down low,
text: Na sor abɔfo beenu no baa Sodom ewimbir; nna Lot tse Sodom abotsen ano: na Lot hun hɔn, na ɔsoɛree kehyiaa hɔn kwan; na ɔkotowee dze n’enyim boom famu;

ref: and said, “Gentlemen, I am your servant. Please come to my home. You can wash your feet, spend the night, and be on your way in the morning.” They told him, “No, we'll spend the night in the city square.”
text: na ɔsee dɛ, Afei hwɛ, mo wuranom, meserɛ hom, wɔmman hom akowaa fie, na wɔnkɔsoɛ hɔ anafua yi nyinara, na hom nhohor hom anan ho, na hom asoɛr anapaatu akɔ hom kwan. Na wɔsee dɛ, Dabida; na yɛbɛtsena gua do anafua yi nyinara.

ref: But Lot kept insisting, until they finally agreed and went home with him. He quickly baked some bread, cooked a meal, and they ate.
text: Na ɔserɛɛ hɔn ɔdzendo ara, na wɔmaan ne nkyɛn, na wɔkɔr ne fie; na ɔtoo hɔn pon, na ɔtoo epitsi ma wodzii.

ref: Before Lot and his guests could go to bed, every man in Sodom, young and old, came and stood outside his house
text: Na wonnyae nnkɛdae no, nna kurow no mu mba, mekyerɛ Sodomfo no, botwaa dan no ho hyiae, mbofra nye mpanyin, apaa nyinara mu nkorɔfo nyinara;

ref: and started shouting, “Where are your visitors? Send them out, so we can have sex with them!”
text: na wɔfrɛɛ Lot na wɔsee no dɛ, Mbarimba a wɔbaa wo nkyɛn anafua yi no wɔ hen? Yi hɔn brɛ hɛn, ma yɛnye hɔn nda.

ref: Lot went outside and shut the door behind him.
text: Na Lot puei kɔr hɔn nkyɛn wɔ abow no ano, na ɔtoow abow no mu wɔ n’ekyir.

ref: Then he said, “Friends, please don't do such a terrible thing!
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Mo nuanom, meserɛ hom, mma wɔnnyɛ emumuyɛ a ɔtse dɛm.

ref: I have two daughters who have never had sex. I'll bring them out, and you can do what you want with them. But don't harm these men. They are guests in my home.”
text: Afei hwɛ, mowɔ mbabaa beenu a wonnhun banyin: meserɛ hom, wɔmma minyi hɔn mbrɛ hom, na dza homara hwɛ a oye no ara hom nyɛ hɔn: mbarimba yi dze mma wɔnnyɛ hɔn biribiara; susuampa dɛ wɔaba me hatsɛw no nwin ase yi.

ref: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Na wɔsee dɛ, Gyina nkyɛn. Na wɔsee dɛ, Akowaa kor yi ɔbaa hɛn amanyɛ, na ɔrohwehwɛ ɔayɛ ɔsɛndzifo: afei yɛnye wo bedzi no bɔn akyɛn hɔn. Na wɔhyɛɛ Lot no do dzendzenndzen, na wɔtwee pinii dɛ worubobubu abow no.

ref: But the two angels in the house reached out and pulled Lot safely inside.
text: Na mbarimba no tseen hɔn nsa, na wɔtwee Lot baa hɔn nkyɛn wɔ dan mu na wɔtoow abow no mu.

ref: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
text: Na wɔdze enyifura baa nkorɔfo a wogyinagyina ofi no n’abow n’ano no hɔn do, nkakraba nye akɛse nyina: ma wɔfonaa dɛ wobohu abow no n’ano kwan. Lot Tu Fi Sodom

ref: Lot went to the men who were engaged to his daughters and said, “Hurry up and get out of here! The Lord is going to destroy this city.” But they thought he was joking, and they laughed at him.
text: Na Lot puei, na ɔkɛkasaa kyerɛɛ ne nsewnom a wɔawarwar ne mbabaa no, na ɔsee dɛ, Hom nsoɛr mfi ha; na EWURADZE rebɛsɛɛ kurow yi. Na ɔyɛɛ ne nsewnom no enyiwa do dɛ obi a oridzi fɛw.

ref: Early the next morning the two angels tried to make Lot hurry and leave. They said, “Take your wife and your two daughters and get away from here as fast as you can! If you don't, every one of you will be killed when the Lord destroys the city.”
text: Na adze kyee no, sor abɔfo no guu Lot do, dɛ, Soɛr, fa wo yer nye wo mbabaa beenu a wɔwɔ ha yi kɔ; amma aannhyew wɔ kurow yi n’emumuyɛ mu.

ref: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
text: Na okyinkyimii; na EWURADZE hun no mbɔbɔr ntsi, nkorɔfo no suoo ɔno nye ne yer nye ne mbabaa beenu no hɔn nsa mu na wɔdze hɔn puei, na wɔdze hɔn kesii kurow no n’ekyir.

ref: When they were outside, one of the angels said, “Run for your lives! Don't even look back. And don't stop in the valley. Run to the hills, where you'll be safe.”
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, wɔdze hɔn puei no, wɔsee dɛ, Hom nguan wɔ hom nkwa ntsi; mma hom mmbu hom kɔn ekyir, mma hom nngyina Tɛtɛr yi mu beebiara so; hom guan nkɔ bepɔw no do, ɔno bɛyɛ na hom ahyew.

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: Afei hwɛ, w’akowaa enya w’enyim enyimpa, na ama w’ehumbɔbɔr a edze ehu me adɔɔ so, wɔ dɛ agye me nkwa; mbom munnkotum meguan akɔ mbepɔw no do, ɔno bɛyɛ na mbusu no ato me ma meewu.

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: Hwɛ, kurow yi bɛn dɛ mubotum eguan akɔ mu, na osuar. Innhu dɛ osuar? Ao, ma munguan nkɔ hɔ, na me kra benya nkwa.

ref: “All right, go there,” he answered. “I won't destroy that town.
text: Na ɔsee no dɛ, Hwɛ, metsie wo wɔ adze kor yi so mu, dɛ monnkɔbɔ kurow a aka ho asɛm no.

ref: Hurry! Run! I can't do anything until you are safely there.” The town was later called Zoar because Lot had said it was small. Sodom and Gomorrah Are Destroyed
text: Ka woho ɛ, guan kɔ hɔ; na murunntum biribiara yɛ, gyedɛ edu hɔ ana. Dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ kurow no ne dzin Zoar ase nye dɛ kakraba.

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
text: Na aber a Lot dur Zoar no, nna ewia epue asaase do.

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Nna EWURADZE tɔɔ brestɔn nye ogya fi EWURADZE hɔ wɔ sor guu Sodom nye Gomora do;

ref: He destroyed those cities and everyone who lived in them, as well as their land and the trees and grass that grew there.
