text: Na Jakob faa sɛsɛ dua nye almɔnd dua nye odwon dua mbaa momon; na oyiyii ho nsema efufuw, na ɔmaa mbaa no ho efufuw no puepuei.

ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: Na ɔdze mbaa no a oyiyii ho no guu nguan no hɔn tsentsen do wɔ nsuka a ɔwɔ mu mbrɛ nguan no ba hɔ bɔnom nsu no mu; na wɔba bɔnom nsu a nna wɔfow hɔnho.

ref: in front of the branches, and their young were spotted and speckled.
text: Na nguan no nyinsenyinsenii wɔ mbaa no enyim, na nguan no wowoo nsensanee nsensanee, woranworan, nye ntokowa ntokowa.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Na Jakob paa nguambaa no na ɔmaa nguan no hɔn enyim kɔr Laban nguan no mu nsensanee nsensanee nye etuntum no nyinara hɔn do; na ɔyɛɛ narankasa no nguankuw wɔ nkyɛn, na ɔammfa ammfora Laban no nguan no mu.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, aber biara sɛ nguan a hɔn ho yɛ dzen no wonyinsenyinsen a, nna Jakob dze mbaa no ekogu nguan no hɔn enyim wɔ nsuka no mu, ama woeenyinsenyinsen mbaa no do;

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: na sɛ nguan no yɛ mberɛw dze a, ɔno ɔmmfa nngu mu: dɛm ntsi hɔn a wɔyɛ mberɛw no nye Laban dze, na hɔn a hɔn ho yɛ dzen no nye Jakob dze.

ref: Jacob soon became rich and successful. He owned many sheep, goats, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of slaves.
text: Na Jakob yɛɛ kɛsenara, na onyaa nguan pii, nye mfonabaa nye nkowaa, nye efumpɔnkɔ nye nsoasopɔnkɔ.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Na ɔtsee Laban ne mbabanyin nsɛm a wɔkae, dɛ, Jakob afa dza ɔyɛ hɛn egya dze nyinara; na dza ɔyɛ hɛn egya dze na ɔdze enya egyapadze yi nyinara.

ref: Jacob also noticed that Laban was not as friendly as he had been before.
text: Na Jakob hwɛɛ Laban no ntɔn mu, na hwɛ, ɔnntse dɛ mbrɛ nkyɛ nkan no ɔtse wɔ no do no.

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Na EWURADZE see Jakob dɛ, San kɔ w’egyanom asaase do, na w’ebusua mu; na mibedzi w’ekyir.

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Na Jakob somae na wɔkɛfrɛɛ Rahel nye Leah ma wɔbaa hamu wɔ no nguan no nkyɛn,

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Muhu hom egya no ntɔn dɛ ɔnntse me dɛ nkan no; na m’egya ne Nyankopɔn na oedzi m’ekyir.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Na homara nyim dɛ m’ahoɔdzen nyinara na medze asom hom egya.

ref: and that he keeps cheating me by changing my wages time after time. But God has protected me.
text: Na hom egya adaadaa me, na ɔasakyer m’akatua mpɛn du; na Nyankopɔn na ɔamma no kwan ma ɔannyɛ me bɔn.

ref: When your father said the speckled sheep would be my wages, all of them were speckled. And when he said the spotted ones would be mine, all of them were spotted.
text: Sɛ ɔse dɛ, Hɔn a hɔn ho yɛ woranworan no na ɔnyɛ w’akatua a, ɔno nguan no nyinara wowo woranworan: na sɛ ɔse dɛ, Hɔn a hɔn ho yɛ nsensanee nsensanee na ɔnyɛ w’akatua a, ɔno nguan no nyinara wowo nsensanee nsensanee.

ref: That's how God has taken sheep and goats from your father and given them to me.
text: Dɛm na Nyankopɔn agye hom egya no mbowa, na ɔdze hɔn ama me.

ref: Then God's angel called me by name. I answered,
text: Na Nyankopɔn no bɔfoe see me wɔ adaaso mu dɛ, Jakob: na mesee dɛ, Emi nye yi.

ref: and he said, “Notice that all the rams are either spotted or speckled. I know everything Laban is doing to you,
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Afei ma w’enyiwa do, na hwɛ, mpɔnkyenyin a wɔfow mpɔnkyeber no nyinara hɔn ho yɛ nsensanee nsensanee, woranworan, nye ntokowa ntokowa: na mehu dza Laban reyɛ wo nyinara.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Emi nye Bɛtɛl Nyankopɔn, beebi mbrɛ eseraa idum ho no a, beebi mbrɛ ebɔɔ me anohoba no a; afei soɛr, fi asaase yi do, na san kɔ w’awobea asaase do.

ref: He treats us like foreigners and has even cheated us out of the bride price that should have been ours.
text: Ana ommbuu hɛn ahɔho bi a? Osiandɛ ɔatɔn hɛn, na eso oedzi hɛn tsisika ewie.

ref: So do whatever God tells you to do. Even the property God took from our father and gave to you really belongs to us and our children.
text: Osiandɛ ahonyadze a Nyankopɔn agye no hɛn egya hɔ nyinara hɛn nye hɛn mba dze a: na afei, dza Nyankopɔn aka akyerɛ wo no nyinara, yɛ.

ref: Then Jacob, his wives, and his children got on camels and left
text: Nna Jakob soɛree, na ɔfaa ne mba nye ne yernom tsenatsenaa efumpɔnkɔ do;

ref: northern Syria for the home of his father Isaac in Canaan. Jacob took along all his flocks, herds, and other property.
text: na ɔfaa no mbowa nyinara, nye n’ahodze a onyae wɔ Paddan-aram no nyinara na, osii mu dɛ ɔrokɔ n’egya Isaak nkyɛn wɔ Keenan asaase do.

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: Nna Laban kɔ rukotwitwa no nguan ho nhwi: na Rahel wiaa n’egya no suman.

ref: long years to earn your two daughters and another
text: Na Jakob wiaa noho guanee; osiandɛ ɔannka annkyerɛ Laban, Sirianyi no.

ref: When Jacob crossed the Euphrates River and headed for the hill country of Gilead, he took with him everything he owned. Laban Catches Up with Jacob
text: Na ɔdze dza ɔwɔ nyinara guanee; na ɔsoɛree, na otwaa Esutsen no, na ɔmaa n’enyi kɔr Gilead bepɔw do. Laban Tsie Jakob

ref: Three days later Laban found out that Jacob had gone.
text: Na ne ndaansa no wɔkaa kyerɛɛ Laban dɛ Jakob eguan.

ref: So he took some of his relatives along and chased after Jacob for seven days, before catching up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
text: Na ɔfaa no nkorɔfo kaa noho, na otsiiw no nda esuon kwan; na ɔbɔtoo no Gilead bepɔw do.

ref: But God appeared to Laban in a dream that night and warned, “Don't say a word to Jacob. Don't make a threat or a promise.”
text: Na Nyankopɔn baa Laban, Sirianyi no hɔ wɔ anafua adaaso mu, na ɔsee no dɛ, Hwɛ woho yie amma aannkasa papa anaa bɔn annkyerɛ Jakob.

ref: Jacob had set up camp in the hill country of Gilead, when Laban and his relatives came and set up camp in another part of the hill country. Laban went to Jacob
text: Na Laban too Jakob. Nna Jakob abɔ n’asesee wɔ bepɔw no do: na Laban ɔnye no nkorɔfo so bobɔɔ hɔndze wɔ Gilead bepɔw do.

ref: and said: Look what you've done! You've tricked me and run off with my daughters like a kidnapper.
text: Na Laban bisaa Jakob dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ayɛ yi, na ewia woho eguan me, na asoa me mbabaa dze hɔn rokɔ dɛ hɔn a wɔdze mfowa afa hɔn ndɔmmum yi?

ref: Why did you sneak away without telling me? I would have given you a going-away party with singing and with music on tambourines and harps.
text: Ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na isumae guanee, na iwiaa woho fii me nkyɛn; na annka annkyerɛ me, ma maammfa enyigye na ndwom, mpintsin na ɔnye nsanku menngya wo kwan;

ref: You didn't even give me a chance to kiss my own grandchildren and daughters goodbye. That was really foolish.
text: na amma maammfew me mbabanyin nye me mbabaa hɔn ano? Afei dze ayɛ nkwaseadze.

ref: I could easily hurt you, but the God your father worshiped has warned me not to make any threats or promises.
text: Mowɔ tum dɛ nkyɛ menye wo dzi no bɔn: na ndeda anafua w’egya ne Nyankopɔn kasaa kyerɛɛ me dɛ, Hwɛ woho yie, mma aannkasa papa anaa bɔn annkyerɛ Jakob.

ref: I can understand why you were eager to return to your father, but why did you have to steal my idols?
text: Na afei kaansa afe w’egya fie dodoodow ma ehia dɛ ekɔ mpo a, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na ewia mo suman yi?

ref: Jacob answered, “I left secretly because I was afraid you would take your daughters from me by force.
text: Na Jakob buae na ɔsee Laban dɛ, Osiandɛ musuroi: na mesee dɛ, ɔbɛyɛ na eregye wo mbabaa wɔ me nsamu enyimpi do.

ref: If you find that any one of us has taken your idols, I'll have that person killed. Let your relatives be witnesses. Show me what belongs to you, and you can take it back.” Jacob did not realize that Rachel had stolen the household idols.
text: Nyimpa biara a ibohu wo suman wɔ no hɔ no, onyii no ɔnnkɛtsena ase: hɛn nuanom hɔn enyim ha yi ara hwehwɛ dza ɔyɛ wodze wɔ me nkyɛn, na fa w’adze. Nna Jakob nnyim dɛ Rahel na oewia.

ref: Laban searched the tents of Jacob, Leah, and the two servant women, but did not find the idols. Then he went to Rachel's tent.
text: Na Laban kɔr Jakob n’asesee mu, nye Leah n’asesee mu, nye mbaawa beenu no hɔn asesee mu; na oennhu. Na ofii Leah n’asesee mu, na ɔkɛhɛɛn Rahel n’asesee mu.

ref: She had already hidden them in the cushion she used as a saddle and was sitting on it. Laban searched everywhere and did not find them.
text: Afei Rahel afa suman no, na ɔdze ahyehyɛ efupɔnkɔ n’adze a wɔdze hyehyɛ no mu, na ɔatsena do. Na Laban kekaa asese no no ho nyinara, na oennhu.

ref: Rachel said, “Father, please don't be angry with me for not getting up; I'm having my period.” Laban kept on searching, but still did not find the idols.
text: Na ɔsee n’egya dɛ, Mma mma ɔnnyɛ mo wura ebufuw dɛ munntum monnsoɛr wɔ w’enyim; na mayɛ mbaadze. Na ɔhwehwɛe, na oennhu suman no.

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Na Jakob no bo fuwii, na ɔnye Laban hamee: na Jakob buae see Laban dɛ, Mo mfom nye mmuana? Mo bɔn nye mmuana, ma etsiw me ɔhyew do dɛm yi?

ref: After searching through everything I have, did you find anything of yours? If so, put it here, where your relatives and mine can see it. Then we can decide what to do.
text: Ehwehwɛɛ me ndzɛmba nyinara yi, wo fie ndzɛmba nyinara mu ebɛnadze na ehu no wɔ mu? Fa si mo nuanom nye wo nuanom hɔn enyim ha, ma wombu hɛn beenu ntamu atsɛn.

ref: If a wild animal killed one of your sheep or goats, I paid for it myself. In fact, you demanded the full price, whether the animal was killed during the day or at night.
text: Hɔn a mbowa kum hɔn no, mammfa ammbrɛ wo; ɔyɛɛ mara me kaw. Dza wowiaa no ewia o, dza wowiaa no anafua o, egyee no mo hɔ.

ref: I sweated every day, and I couldn't sleep at night because of the cold.
text: Mbrɛ meyɛe nye yi; adzekyee a nna ewia rehyew me, adzesaa a nna awɔw resar me; na m’enyi so kokwae.

ref: If the fearsome God worshiped by Abraham and my father Isaac had not been on my side, you would have sent me away without a thing. But God saw my hard work, and he knew the trouble I was in, so he helped me. Then last night he told you how wrong you were. Jacob and Laban Make an Agreement
text: Sɛ m’egya ne Nyankopɔn, Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, nye Isaak no Suro, enndzi m’ekyir a, nkyɛ ndɛ egya me kwan nsapan. Nyankopɔn ehu m’amandzehu nye me nsaano edwumayɛ, na anadwe yi ɔaka w’enyim. Jakob Na Laban Dzi Ahyɛmu

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Na Laban buae na ɔsee Jakob dɛ, Mbabaa yi me mbabaa a, na mba yi me mba a, na nguan yi mo nguan a, na dza ihu yi nyinara medze a: na ebɛnadze na mubotum mayɛ me mbabaa yi, anaa hɔn mba a wɔawo hɔn yi ndɛ?

ref: So I am ready to make an agreement with you, and we will pile up some large rocks here to remind us of the agreement.”
text: Na afei bra, ma yendzi ahyɛmu, emi nye ɔwo; na ma ɔnyɛ dase wɔ emi nye ɔwo ntamu.

ref: After Jacob had set up a large rock,
text: Na Jakob faa ɔbo, na ɔdze yɛɛ idum sii hɔ.

ref: he told his men to get some more rocks and pile them up next to it. Then Jacob and Laban ate a meal together beside the rocks.
text: Na Jakob see no nkorɔfo dɛ, Hom mboaboa abo ano; na wɔtasee abo, na wɔhɔɔr no isiw: na wodzidzii siw no ho hɔ.

ref: Laban named the pile of rocks Jegar Sahadutha. But Jacob named it Galeed.
text: Na Laban frɛɛ no Jegar-sahaduta: na Jakob dze ɔfrɛɛ no Galeed.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “This pile of rocks will remind us of our agreement.” That's why the place was named Galeed.
text: Na Laban see dɛ, Isiw yi nyɛ emi nye ɔwo ntamu dase ndɛ. Dɛm ntsi ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Galeed:

ref: Laban also said, “This pile of rocks means that the Lord will watch us both while we are apart from each other.” So the place was also named Mizpah.
text: na Mizpa osiandɛ ɔsee dɛ, sɛ Yɛpaapaa mu a, EWURADZE nhwɛ emi nye ɔwo ntamu.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Sɛ ebɛtseetsee me mbabaa, na sɛ ebɔwar yernom aka me mbabaa ho a, obi nnka hɛn ho; hwɛ, Nyankopɔn nye emi nye ɔwo ntamu dasefo.

ref: Both this pile of rocks and this large rock have been set up between us as a reminder. I must never go past them to attack you, and you must never come past them to attack me.
text: Isiw yi yɛ dase, na idum yi yɛ dase, dɛ mennkɛtra siw yi maba wo nkyɛn, na wo so ennkɛtra siw yi nye idum yi aba me nkyɛn, abɛyɛ bɔn.

ref: My father Nahor, your grandfather Abraham, and their ancestors all worshiped the same God, and he will make sure that we each keep the agreement. Then Jacob made a promise in the name of the fearsome God his father Isaac had worshiped.
text: Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, nye Nahor Nyankopɔn, hɔn egya ne Nyankopɔn, ombu hɛn ntamu atsɛn. Na Jakob dze n’egya Isaak no Suro no dzii nsew.

ref: Jacob killed an animal and offered it as a sacrifice there on the mountain, and he invited his men to eat with him. After the meal they spent the night on the mountain.
text: Na Jakob bɔɔ afɔr wɔ bepɔw no do, na ɔfrɛɛ no nuanom dɛ wombedzidzi: na wodzidzii, na wotsintsimii wɔ bepɔw no do anafua no nyinara.

ref: Early the next morning, Laban kissed his daughters and his grandchildren goodbye, then he left to go back home.
text: Na Laban soɛɛr anapaatu, na ɔfeew ne mbabanyin nye ne mbabaa no hɔn ano, na ohyiraa hɔn: na Laban sanee kɔr no kurow mu.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Na Jakob sii no kwan do, na Nyankopɔn n’abɔfoe behyiaa no.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Jakob hun hɔn no ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn no dɔm nye yi: na ɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Mahanaim.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Na Jakob tuu abɔfo ma wodzii n’enyim kan kɔr no nua Esau hɔ wɔ Seir asaase do, Edom haban mu.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ hɔn dɛ, Hom nkɛse mo wura Esau dɛm; dɛ, Mbrɛ w’akowaa Jakob se nye yi, Menye Laban akɛtsena, na makyɛr hɔ ara ma ebodu ndɛ.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Menya anantwi, nsoasopɔnkɔ, nguan, mpɔnkye na nkowaa nye mfonabaa: na masoma dɛ wɔmbɛka nkyerɛ mo wura, ama meenya w’enyim adom.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Na abɔfo no sanee baa Jakob hɔ, dɛ, Yɛkɔr wo nua Esau hɔ na mbom ɔnoara so reba ebehyia wo kwan, na mbarimba ahaanan ka no ho.

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Nna Jakob n’akoma tuu kɛsenara, na no ho hiahiaa no. Na obuee nkorɔfo a wɔnye no nam no, nye mpɔnkye, nguan, anantwi nye efumpɔnkɔ no hɔn mu ekuw ebien;

ref: He thought, “If Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.”
text: na ɔsee dɛ, Sɛ Esau ba kuw kor do, na oboku hɔn a, ɔno ikuw a ɔaka no eefi nsa.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Na Jakob see dɛ, Ao, m’egya Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, na m’egya Isaak ne Nyankopɔn, ao, EWURADZE a esee me dɛ, San kɔ wo man mu, nye w’ebusua mu, na mebɛyɛ wo yie:

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: Meserɛ wo, gye me fi mo nua Esau nsamu: na musuro no, bɛyɛ na ɔaba eboku emi, nye enanom nye hɔn mba yi.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Na esee me dɛ, Ampaara mebɛyɛ wo yie, na mama w’asefo ayɛ dɛ mpoano anhwea a no dodow ntsi wɔkan a ɔnnyɛ.

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Na ɔsoɛɛ hɔ anafua no; na oyii dza ɔwɔ ne nkyɛn no mu bi dɛ ɔdze rekɛkyɛ no nua Esau;

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Na hom nse dɛ, Nkyii so, hwɛ w’akowaa Jakob so dzi hɛn ekyir. Afei ɔkaa ɔsee dɛ, Medze akyɛdze a odzi m’enyim yi bɔkɔ akɔdwedwe no, na n’ekyir no meehu n’enyim; bi a no bo bɔdwe.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Jakob frɛɛ bea no ne dzin Peniel; osiandɛ, ɔsee dɛ, Mehu Nyankopɔn mpu na mpu, naaso me kra enya nkwa.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: Na ɔdze no ekuwekuw hyɛɛ no nkowaa no nsa; na ɔsee no nkowaa no dɛ, Hom ntwa mu ndzi m’enyim, na hom mma ikuw nye ikuw ntamu mbra kwan.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ dza odzi kan no dɛ, Sɛ mo nua Esau nye wo hyia, na obisa wo dɛ, Woana n’abofra nye wo, erokɔ henfa, na dza wodzi w’enyim yi woana dze a?

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ɔno se dɛ, W’akowaa Jakob dze a; ɔyɛ akyɛdze a wɔdze rekɛma mo wura Esau: na, hwɛ, ɔnoara so dzi hɛn ekyir.

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ dza otsia ebien no so, nye dza otsia ebiasa no, nye hɔn a wɔka mbowakuw no do no nyinara dɛ, Sɛ hom hu Esau a, wɔnka dɛm asɛm yi ara nkyerɛ no.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Nna akyɛdze no twaa mu dzii n’enyim: na ɔnoara dze, ɔtsenaa dɔm no mu anafua no. Jakob N’aperdzi Wɔ Peniel

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Na ɔkaa Jakob no nko; na banyin bi nye no dzii aper wɔ hɔ ara ma okodur adzekyee.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na nyimpa no hun dɛ onndzi Jakob do konyim no, nna ɔkaa n’aserɛ tokur mu; na Jakob no ndwonku huanee wɔ aperper a ɔnye no dzii no mu.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Na banyin no see dɛ, Ma monkɔ, na adze rekye. Na ɔsee dɛ, Mennkɛma akɔ, gyedɛ ehyira me ana.

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Na banyin no bisaa no dɛ, Wo dzin dze dɛn? Na obuaa dɛ, Jakob.

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Wɔnnkɛfrɛ wo dzin Jakob bio, na mbom, Israel: osiandɛ ɔwo enye Nyankopɔn na ɔnye nyimpa ako, na edzi konyim.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Na Jakob bisaa no dɛ, Meserɛ wo, bɔ wo dzin kyerɛ me. Na ɔsee dɛ, Na iribisa me dzin ayɛ dɛn? Na banyin no hyiraa no wɔ hɔ.

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Na orutwa mu wɔ Peniel no nna ewia epue, na no ndwonku a ehuan no ntsi nna ohuan n’aserɛ mu.

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Dɛm ntsi ɔdze bodu ndɛ da yi ara Israelmba nndzi ntsin a ɔwɔ ndwonku mu no: osiandɛ ɔkaa Jakob no ndwonku wɔ mbrɛ ntsin no wɔ no.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Na Jakob sii no kwan do, na Nyankopɔn n’abɔfoe behyiaa no.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Jakob hun hɔn no ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn no dɔm nye yi: na ɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Mahanaim.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Na Jakob tuu abɔfo ma wodzii n’enyim kan kɔr no nua Esau hɔ wɔ Seir asaase do, Edom haban mu.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ hɔn dɛ, Hom nkɛse mo wura Esau dɛm; dɛ, Mbrɛ w’akowaa Jakob se nye yi, Menye Laban akɛtsena, na makyɛr hɔ ara ma ebodu ndɛ.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Menya anantwi, nsoasopɔnkɔ, nguan, mpɔnkye na nkowaa nye mfonabaa: na masoma dɛ wɔmbɛka nkyerɛ mo wura, ama meenya w’enyim adom.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Na abɔfo no sanee baa Jakob hɔ, dɛ, Yɛkɔr wo nua Esau hɔ na mbom ɔnoara so reba ebehyia wo kwan, na mbarimba ahaanan ka no ho.

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Nna Jakob n’akoma tuu kɛsenara, na no ho hiahiaa no. Na obuee nkorɔfo a wɔnye no nam no, nye mpɔnkye, nguan, anantwi nye efumpɔnkɔ no hɔn mu ekuw ebien;

ref: He thought, “If Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.”
text: na ɔsee dɛ, Sɛ Esau ba kuw kor do, na oboku hɔn a, ɔno ikuw a ɔaka no eefi nsa.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Na Jakob see dɛ, Ao, m’egya Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, na m’egya Isaak ne Nyankopɔn, ao, EWURADZE a esee me dɛ, San kɔ wo man mu, nye w’ebusua mu, na mebɛyɛ wo yie:

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: Meserɛ wo, gye me fi mo nua Esau nsamu: na musuro no, bɛyɛ na ɔaba eboku emi, nye enanom nye hɔn mba yi.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Na esee me dɛ, Ampaara mebɛyɛ wo yie, na mama w’asefo ayɛ dɛ mpoano anhwea a no dodow ntsi wɔkan a ɔnnyɛ.

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Na ɔsoɛɛ hɔ anafua no; na oyii dza ɔwɔ ne nkyɛn no mu bi dɛ ɔdze rekɛkyɛ no nua Esau;

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Na hom nse dɛ, Nkyii so, hwɛ w’akowaa Jakob so dzi hɛn ekyir. Afei ɔkaa ɔsee dɛ, Medze akyɛdze a odzi m’enyim yi bɔkɔ akɔdwedwe no, na n’ekyir no meehu n’enyim; bi a no bo bɔdwe.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Jakob frɛɛ bea no ne dzin Peniel; osiandɛ, ɔsee dɛ, Mehu Nyankopɔn mpu na mpu, naaso me kra enya nkwa.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: Na ɔdze no ekuwekuw hyɛɛ no nkowaa no nsa; na ɔsee no nkowaa no dɛ, Hom ntwa mu ndzi m’enyim, na hom mma ikuw nye ikuw ntamu mbra kwan.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ dza odzi kan no dɛ, Sɛ mo nua Esau nye wo hyia, na obisa wo dɛ, Woana n’abofra nye wo, erokɔ henfa, na dza wodzi w’enyim yi woana dze a?

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ɔno se dɛ, W’akowaa Jakob dze a; ɔyɛ akyɛdze a wɔdze rekɛma mo wura Esau: na, hwɛ, ɔnoara so dzi hɛn ekyir.

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ dza otsia ebien no so, nye dza otsia ebiasa no, nye hɔn a wɔka mbowakuw no do no nyinara dɛ, Sɛ hom hu Esau a, wɔnka dɛm asɛm yi ara nkyerɛ no.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Nna akyɛdze no twaa mu dzii n’enyim: na ɔnoara dze, ɔtsenaa dɔm no mu anafua no. Jakob N’aperdzi Wɔ Peniel

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Na ɔkaa Jakob no nko; na banyin bi nye no dzii aper wɔ hɔ ara ma okodur adzekyee.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na nyimpa no hun dɛ onndzi Jakob do konyim no, nna ɔkaa n’aserɛ tokur mu; na Jakob no ndwonku huanee wɔ aperper a ɔnye no dzii no mu.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.

ref: The two servant women, Zilpah and Bilhah, together with their children went first, followed by Leah and her children, then by Rachel and Joseph.
text: Na ɔdze mbaawa no nye hɔn mba no dzii kan, na ɔdze Leah nye ne mba no dzii hɔ, na ɔdze Rahel nye Josef dzii ekyir.

ref: Jacob himself walked in front of them all, bowing to the ground seven times as he came near his brother.
text: Na ɔnoara paa ho kedzii hɔn enyim, na ɔbɔɔ no mu adze mpɛn esuon, dze kodur dɛ ɔbɛɛn no nua no.

ref: But Esau ran toward Jacob and hugged and kissed him. Then the two brothers started crying.
text: Na Esau tutuu enguan hyiaa no kwan, na ɔyɛɛ no etuu; na ɔdze ne nsa too no kɔn mu, na ɔfeew n’ano: na wosui.

ref: When Esau noticed the women and children he asked, “Whose children are these?” Jacob answered, “These are the children the Lord has been kind enough to give to me, your servant.”
text: Na Esau maa n’enyiwa do, na ohun mbaa no nye mbofra no; na obisaa dɛ, Woananom na wɔka wo ho yi? Na Jakob buaa dɛ, Mbofra a Nyankopɔn dze adom w’akowaa a.

ref: Then the two servant women and their children came and bowed down to Esau.
text: Nna mbaawa no nye hɔn mba twee pinii, na wɔbɔɔ hɔn mu adze.

ref: Next, Leah and her children came and bowed down; finally, Joseph and Rachel also came and bowed down.
text: Na Leah nye ne mba so twee pinii, na wɔbɔɔ hɔn mu adze: na n’ekyir no nna Josef nye Rahel twee pinii, na wɔbɔɔ hɔn mu adze.

ref: Esau asked Jacob, “Why did you send those herds I met along the road?” “Master,” Jacob answered, “I sent them so you would be friendly to me.”
text: Na Esau bisaa dɛ, Na ekuw a menye hɔn hyiae yi nyinara n’ase nye dɛn? Na ɔsee dɛ, Medze robɔhwehwɛ mo wura n’enyim enyimnyam.

ref: “But, brother, I already have plenty,” Esau replied. “Keep them for yourself.”
text: Na Esau see dɛ, Dza mowɔ no ara dɔɔ so; mo nua, ma w’adze nko hɔ mma wo.

ref: “No!” Jacob said. “Please accept them as a sign of your friendship for me. When you welcomed me and I saw your face, it was like seeing the face of God.
text: Na Jakob see dɛ, Dabida, meserɛ wo, sɛ afei menya w’enyim enyimnyam a, nna gye me nsamu akyɛdze: susuampa dɛ mehu w’enyim ara dɛ mbrɛ obiara bohu Nyankopɔn enyim, na masɔ w’enyi yi.

ref: Please accept these as gifts I brought to you. God has been good to me, and I have everything I need.” Jacob kept insisting until Esau agreed.
text: Meserɛ wo, gye m’akyɛdze a wɔdze abrɛ wo yi; osiandɛ Nyankopɔn adom me, na eso dza mowɔ no dɔɔ so. Na ɔhyɛɛ no ma ɔgyee.

ref: “Let's get ready to travel,” Esau said. “I'll go along with you.”
text: Na Esau see dɛ, Ma yensi mu, na yɛnkɔ, na mibedzi w’enyim.

ref: But Jacob answered, “Master, you know traveling is hard on children, and I have to look after the sheep and goats that are nursing their young. If my animals travel too much in one day, they will all die.
text: Na ɔsee no dɛ, Mo wura nyim dɛ mbofra yi yɛ mberɛw, na eso nguan nye anantwi a wɔnye me nam yi wɔma enumfo: na sɛ wɔka hɔn dodow da kor a, nguan no nyinara bowuwu.

ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”
text: Meserɛ wo, wɔmma mo wura mpa ho nkedzi n’akowaa enyim: na emi mobɔdwe m’enyi ara manantsew dɛ mbrɛ mbowa a wodzi m’enyim yi botum, na dɛ mbrɛ mbofra no botum, ekodu dɛ mubodu mo wura nkyɛn wɔ Seir.

ref: Esau replied, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “You don't have to do that,” Jacob answered. “I am happy, simply knowing that you are friendly to me.”
text: Na Esau see dɛ, Nna ma memfa nyimpa a wɔka mo ho yi bi mingya wo. Na ɔsee dɛ, Ma ɔnyɛ dɛn? Wɔmma minnya mo wura n’enyim enyimnyam.

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem
text: Na Jakob sii mu kɔr Sukɔt, na okesii dan maa noho, na osisii mpata maa no mbowa: dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Sukɔt.

ref: After leaving northern Syria, Jacob arrived safely at Shechem in Canaan and set up camp outside the city.
text: Na Jakob baa Sekem kurow a ɔwɔ Keenan asaase do no mu asomdwee mu, wɔ ber a ofii Paddan-aram bae no; na ɔbɔbɔɔ asese wɔ kurow no n’enyim.

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: Na ɔtɔɔ asaase kakraba a ɔbɔɔ n’asesee wɔ do no, wɔ Sekem n’egya Hamor ne mba no hɔ, maa dwetɛ ɔha.

ref: then he set up his tents and built an altar there to honor the God of Israel.
text: Na osii afɔrmbukyia wɔ hɔ, na ɔfrɛɛ no El-elohe-Israel a asekyerɛ nye Israel Nyankopɔn.

ref: Dinah, the daughter of Jacob and Leah, went to visit some of the women who lived nearby.
text: Na Dina, Leah na Jakob hɔn babaa no, puei dɛ ɔrokɔhwɛ asaase no do mbabaa.

ref: She was seen by Hamor's son Shechem, the leader of the Hivites, and he grabbed her and raped her.
text: Na Hamor, Hivinyi no, asaase no do hen no ne ba Sekem hun no; na ɔkyeer no nye no dae, na ɔbrɛɛ no adze.

ref: But Shechem was attracted to Dinah, so he told her how much he loved her.
text: Na no werɛ kitsaa Dina Jakob ne babaa no, na ɔdɔɔ akataasia no na ɔkasaa ayamuyie mu kyerɛɛ no.

ref: Shechem even asked his father to arrange for him to marry her.
text: Na Sekem kaa kyerɛɛ n’egya Hamor dɛ, Kɛgye akataasia yi yer ma me.

ref: Meanwhile, Jacob heard what had happened. But his sons were out in the fields with the cattle, so he did not do anything at the time.
text: Afei Jakob tsee dɛ oegu ne babaa Dina ho fi. Nna ne mbabanyin no wɔ no mbowa nkyɛn wɔ hamu ntsi ɔyɛɛ komm ma wɔbae ana.

ref: just as Jacob's sons were coming in from work. When they learned that their sister had been raped, they became furiously angry, because nothing is more disgraceful than rape, and it must not be tolerated.
text: Na Jakob ne mbabanyin no tsee no, wofii hamu baa fie: na ɔyɛɛ hɔn no yaw, na hɔn bo fuuw kɛsenara, osiandɛ Sekem ayɛ nkwaseadze wɔ Israel dɛ ɔnye Jakob ne babaa ada; adze a wɔnnyɛ.

ref: Hamor said to Jacob and his sons: My son Shechem really loves Dinah. Please let him marry her.
text: Na Hamor nye hɔn twetwee nkɔmbɔ see dɛ, Me ba Sekem no werɛ ekitsa hom babaa: meserɛ hom, wɔmfa no mma no awar.

ref: Why don't you start letting your families marry into our families and ours marry into yours?
text: Na hom nye hɛn ndzi awarwar; hom mfa hom mbabaa mma hɛn, na hom so mfa hɛn mbabaa.

ref: You can share this land with us. Move freely about until you find the property you want; then buy it and settle down here.
text: Na hom nye hɛn ntsena: na asaase a ɔda hom enyim yi hom ntsena do, na hom ndzi gua wɔ do, na hom nnya adze wɔ do.

ref: Shechem added, “Do this favor for me, and I'll give whatever you want.
text: Na Sekem see n’egya nye no nuanom no dɛ, Wɔmma minnya hom enyim enyimnyam, na dza hom bɛse me no mebɛma.

ref: Ask anything, no matter how expensive. I'll do anything, just let me marry Dinah.”
text: Hom mbisa me tsiadze nye akyɛdze kɛse biara, na dza hom bɛse me no ara mebɛma: na hom mfa akataasia no mma me awar.

ref: Jacob's sons wanted to get even with Shechem and his father because of what had happened to their sister.
text: Na Jakob ne mbabanyin no buaa Sekem nye n’egya Hamor ndaadaa mu, na ɔnam dɛ oegu hɔn akyerɛbaa Dina ho fi ntsi.

ref: So they tricked them by saying: You're not circumcised! It would be a disgrace for us to let you marry Dinah now.
text: Na wɔsee hɔn dɛ, Yɛyɛ dɛm adze yi a ɔnnkɛyɛ, dɛ yɛdze hɛn akyerɛbaa bɛma obi a wonntwaa no twatsia; na dɛm no yɛ enyimguase ma hɛn.

ref: But we will let you marry her, if you and the other men in your tribe agree to be circumcised.
text: Dza ogyina do a yɛbɛgye hom pen ara nye yi: dɛ sɛ hom bɛyɛ dɛ mbrɛ yɛtse, na woetwa hom mu mbanyin nyinara twatsia a;

ref: Then your families can marry into ours, and ours can marry into yours, and we can live together like one nation.
text: yɛdze hɛn mbabaa bɛma hom, na hɛn so yɛafa hom mbabaa, na yɛnye hom atsena, na yɛayɛ ɔman kor.

ref: But if you don't agree to be circumcised, we'll take Dinah and leave this place.
text: Na sɛ hom nnketsie hɛn, na hom etwitwa twatsia a; ɔno yɛbɛfa hɛn babaa, na yɛakɔ.

ref: Hamor and Shechem liked what was said.
text: Na hɔn nsɛm no yɛɛ Hamor nye Hamor ne ba Sekem hɔn pɛ.

ref: Shechem was the most respected person in his family, and he was so in love with Dinah that he hurried off to get everything done.
text: Na aberantsɛ no ennkyinkyim dɛ ɔbɛyɛ dɛm adze no, osiandɛ ɔpɛ Jakob ne babaa no. Nna wodzi Sekem nyi sen n’egya ne fifo nyinara.

ref: The two men met with the other leaders of their city and told them:
text: Na Hamor nye ne ba Sekem baa hɔn kurow n’abotsenano, na wɔnye hɔn kurow mu mbanyin no twetwee nkɔmbɔ, dɛ,

ref: These people really are friendly. Why not let them move freely about until they find the property they want? There's enough land here for them and for us. Then our families can marry into theirs, and theirs can marry into ours.
text: Mbarimba yi wɔnye hɛn tse no asomdwee mu; dɛm ntsi hom mma wɔntsena asaase yi do, na wondzi mu gua; na, hwɛ asaase yi dɔɔ so ara yie ma hɔn; hom mma yɛmfa hɔn mbabaa nwar hɔn, na wɔmma hɛn so yɛmfa hɛn mbabaa mma hɔn.

ref: We have to do only one thing before they will agree to stay here and become one nation with us. Our men will have to be circumcised just like theirs.
text: Na adze ara a ogyina do a mbanyin no bɛgye hɛn pen na wɔnye hɛn atsena, ma yaayɛ ɔman kor nye dɛ, hɛn mu mbanyin nyinara botwitwa twatsia, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn woetwitwa no.

ref: Just think! We'll get their property, as well as their flocks and herds. All we have to do is to agree, and they will live here with us.
text: Ana nkyɛ hɔn anyɛmbowa nye hɔn ahodze nye hɔn mbowa nyinara nnkɛyɛ hɛndze bi a? Hom mma yɛngye hɔn pen ara, na wɔnye hɛn bɛtsena.

ref: Three days later the men who had been circumcised were still weak from pain. So Simeon and Levi, two of Dinah's brothers, attacked with their swords and killed every man in the town,
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ ne ndaansa no a ne yaw kitsaa hɔn no, nna Jakob ne mbabanyin beenu, Simeon nye Levi, Dina no nuanom no, wɔfefaa hɔn mfowa, na wokefiir kurow no awora, na wokunkum mbanyin no nyinara.

ref: Jacob's other sons came and took everything they wanted. All this was done because of the horrible thing that had happened to their sister.
text: Jakob ne mbabanyin no baa atɔfo no do na wɔbɔfoom kurow no mu ndzɛmba, osiandɛ woegu hɔn akyerɛbaa no ho fi.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.
text: Wɔfaa hɔn nguan nye hɔn anantwi, nye hɔn nsoasopɔnkɔ nye dza ɔwɔ kurow no mu, na dza ɔwɔ haban mu;

ref: After taking everything of value from the houses, they dragged away the wives and children of their victims.
text: na hɔn ahodze nyinara. Wɔfaa hɔn mbofraba nyinara nye hɔn yernom ndɔmmum, na wɔfoom hɔn, mekyerɛ dza ɔwɔ fie nyinara.

ref: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “Look what you've done! Now I'm in real trouble with the Canaanites and Perizzites who live around here. There aren't many of us, and if they attack, they'll kill everyone in my household.”
text: Na Jakob see Simeon nye Levi dɛ, Hom afona me, dɛ hom ama mo ho abɔn wɔ hɔn a wɔtse asaase yi do hɔn mu, wɔ Keenanfo nye Perizfo hɔn mu: na mo ho suar ntsi, wɔbɔboaboa hɔn ano aba mo do, na woeboku me; na wɔbɛsɛɛ me, emi nye me fi.

ref: They answered, “Was it right to let our own sister be treated that way?”
text: Na wɔsee dɛ, Na nkyɛ ɔnyɛ hɛn akyerɛbaa dɛ edwaman a?

ref: Afterwards, we'll go to Bethel. I will build an altar there for God, who answered my prayers when I was in trouble and who has always been at my side.
text: na hom mma yɛnsoɛr, na yɛnkɔ Bɛtɛl; na mebɛyɛ afɔrmbukyia wɔ hɔ ama Nyankopɔn a m’ahohia da no ɔgyee me do, na odzii m’ekyir wɔ kwan a mutui no mu no.

ref: So everyone gave Jacob their idols and their earrings, and he buried them under the oak tree near Shechem.
text: Na wɔdze hɔn enyame ananaben a ɔwɔ hɔn nsamu nyinara, nye nkaba a ɔhyehyɛ hɔn asomu no maa Jakob; na Jakob dze hyɛɛ esudur no a ɔwɔ Sekem ho no ase.

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.
text: Na wɔkɔree: na ihu kɛsenara tɔɔ nkurow a wɔabɔ hɔn ho aprɔw no do, ma woenntsiw Jakob ne mbabanyin no.

ref: Jacob built an altar there and called it “God of Bethel,” because that was the place where God had appeared to him when he was running from Esau.
text: Na osii afɔrmbukyia wɔ hɔ, na ɔfrɛɛ bea no El-Bɛtɛl: osiandɛ hɔ na Nyankopɔn yii noho kyerɛɛ no wɔ aber a ofii no nua enyim guanee no.

ref: While they were there, Rebekah's personal servant Deborah died. They buried her under an oak tree and called it “Weeping Oak.” God Blesses Jacob at Bethel
text: Na Debora, Rebeka no hwɛfo no wui, na wosiee no Bɛtɛl anaafo, wɔ esudur no ase: na wɔtoo ne dzin Allon-bakut a asekyerɛ nye Nyinsuwakyi bea.

ref: I will give you the land that I promised Abraham and Isaac, and it will belong to your family forever.
text: Asaase a medze memaa Abraham nye Isaak no, ɔwo na medze mebɛma wo, na w’asefo a wodzi w’ekyir no na medze asaase no bɛma hɔn.

ref: Jacob set up a large rock, so that he would remember what had happened there. Then he poured wine and olive oil on the rock to show that it was dedicated to God,
text: Na Jakob sii idum wɔ mbrɛ ɔnye no kasae no; ɔbo dum a: na ohuee ɔnom afɔr guu do, na ohuee ngo guu do.

ref: Jacob and his family had left Bethel and were still a long way from Ephrath, when the time came for Rachel's baby to be born.
text: Na wofii Bɛtɛl sii mu; na aka ɔkwan kakra bi ma woedu Efrat no: awoo kaa Rahel, na n’awoo no mu yɛɛ dzen.

ref: She was having a rough time, but the woman who was helping her said, “Don't worry! It's a boy.”
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a n’awoo no mu yɛɛ dzen no, nna awogyefo no see no dɛ, Mma nnsuro; ibenya babanyin kor so afei.

ref: Rachel was at the point of death, and right before dying, she said, “I'll name him Benoni.” But Jacob called him Benjamin.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, ne kra rifi no mu no , ɔtoo no dzin Ben-oni; na n’egya dze ɔfrɛɛ no Benjamin.

ref: Rachel was buried beside the road to Ephrath, which is also called Bethlehem.
text: Na Rahel wui, na wosiee no wɔ Efrat kwan mu, a ɔnoara nye Bɛtlɛhɛm.

ref: Jacob set up a tombstone over her grave, and it is still there.
text: Na Jakob sii ne nda do idum: ɔnoara nye Rahel ne nda do idum dze bodu ndɛ da yi.

ref: Jacob, also known as Israel, traveled to the south of Eder Tower, where he set up camp.
text: Na Israel tui, na ɔkɔbɔɔ n’asesee wɔ Eder abantsentsen ekyir noho.

ref: During their time there, Jacob's oldest son Reuben slept with Bilhah, who was one of Jacob's other wives. And Jacob found out about it. Jacob's Twelve Sons
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, Israel tse asaase no do no, nna Reuben kɔree na ɔnye n’egya n’abaawayer Bilha kɛdae: na Israel tsee. Jakob Ne Mbabanyin (

ref: Jacob went to his father Isaac at Hebron, also called Mamre or Kiriath-Arba, where Isaac's father Abraham had lived as a foreigner.
text: Na Jakob baa n’egya Isaak nkyɛn wɔ Mamre, wɔ Kiriat-arba , mbrɛ Abraham nye Isaak bɛtsenae no.

ref: He married three Canaanite women: The first was Adah, the daughter of Elon the Hittite; the second was Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and the granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite;
text: Esau waar ne yernom wɔ Keenan mbabaa mu; Hetnyi Elon ne babaa Ada, nye Hivinyi Zibeon ne babaa Ana ne babaa Oholibama;

ref: the third was Basemath, who was Ishmael's daughter and Nebaioth's sister.
text: na Ismael ne babaa Basemat, Nebaiot n’akyerɛbaa no.

ref: Esau and his three wives had five sons while in Canaan. Adah's son was Eliphaz; Basemath's son was Reuel; Oholibamah's three sons were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: na Oholibama woo Jeus, Jalam, nye Kora: woyinom nye Esau ne mba a wɔwoo hɔn wɔ Keenan asaase do no.

ref: Esau took his children and wives, his relatives and servants, his animals and possessions he had acquired while in Canaan, and moved far from Jacob.
text: Na Esau faa ne yernom, ne mbabanyin, ne mbabaa, ne fie nyimpa nyinara, nye fiembowa, nye no mbowa nyinara, nye n’enyadze a onyaa no wɔ Keenan asaase do nyinara; na ɔtwee noho wɔ no nua Jakob ho kɔr asaase bi do.

ref: He did this because the land was too crowded and could not support him and his brother with their flocks and herds.
text: Osiandɛ hɔn ahodze so dodow ma hɔn dɛ wɔbɛtsena fakor. Na asaase a wɔtse do no wɔnnsen do wɔ hɔn mbowa no ntsi.

ref: That's why Esau made his home in the hill country of Seir.
text: Na Esau a noara so nye Edom no tsenaa Seir bepɔw do.

ref: Esau and Adah's oldest son was Eliphaz, and the clans that descended from him were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz,
text: Woyinom nye Esau ne mba mu ekunyin: Esau n’abakan Elifaz ne mba; ekunyin Teman, Omar, Zefo, Kenaz,

ref: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek. These and Esau's other descendants lived in the land of Edom.
text: Kora, Gatam, na Amalek. Woyinom nye ekunyin a wofi Elifaz no mu puei wɔ Edom asaase do; na woyinom nye Ada n’asefo.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: Na woyinom nye Esau ne yer Oholibama ne mba; ekunyin Jeus, Jalam, na okunyin Kora. Woyinom nye ekunyin a wofi Esau ne yer Oholibama, Ana ne babaa no no mu puei.

ref: All of these clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom.
text: Woyinom nye Esau a ɔnoara nye Edom no n’asefo, na woyinom nye hɔn ekunyin. Seir Asefo (

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Woyinom nye Hornyi Seir ne mba a nna wɔyɛ asaase no do mba no. Lotan, Sobal, Zibeon, Ana,

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Na Lotan ne mba nye Hori na Heman. Nna Lotan n’akyerɛbaa nye Timna.

ref: Shobal's sons were Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
text: Na Sobal ne mba nye woyinom; Alvan, Manahat, Ebal, Sefo nye Onam.

ref: Zibeon's sons were Aiah and Anah—the same Anah who found an oasis in the desert while taking the donkeys of his father out to pasture.
text: Na woyinom nye Zibeon ne mba; Aia nye Ana: oyi nye Ana a, mber a ɔhwɛ n’egya Zibeon no nsoasopɔnkɔ no okohun nsuhyew mpumpunadze wɔ sar do no.

ref: Dishon's sons were Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
text: Na woyinom nye Dison ne mba; Hemdan, Esban, Itran nye Keran.

ref: Ezer's sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text: Ezer ne mba nye woyinom; Bilhan, Zaavan nye Akan.

ref: Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
text: Dison ne mba nye woyinom: Uz nye Aran.

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Ekunyin a wofi Horfo mu puei nye woyinom; ekunyin Lotan, Sobal, Zibeon, na Ana,

ref: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, and they lived in the land of Seir.
text: Dison, Ezer, na Disan. Ekunyin a wofi Horfo mu puei nye woyinom dɛ mbrɛ hɔn ekunyinyɛ tsetsee wɔ Seir asaase do. Edom Ahemfo (

ref: The clans that descended from Esau took their names from their families and the places where they lived. They are Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
text: Na ekunyin a wofi Esau mu puei hɔn edzin nye yi; dɛ mbrɛ hɔn ebusua ebusua, hɔn tsenabew, nye hɔn edzin tsetsee, ekunyin Timna, Alva, Jetet;

ref: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
text: Oholibama, Ela na okunyin Pinon;

ref: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
text: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar;

ref: Magdiel, and Iram. These clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom, the father of the Edomites. They took their names from the places where they settled.
text: Magdiel, Iram. Woyinom nye Edom ekunyin, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn tsenabew tsetsee wɔ asaase a ɔyɛ hɔn enyadze no do. Oyi nye Esau, Edomfo hɔn egya no.

ref: Jacob lived in the land of Canaan, where his father Isaac had lived,
text: Na Jakob tsenaa asaase a n’egya tsenaa do no do, Keenan asaase do.

ref: “They're not here anymore,” the man replied. “I overheard them say they were going to Dothan.” Joseph left and found his brothers in Dothan.
text: Na banyin no see dɛ, Woefi ha kɔ: na metsee dɛ wɔreka dɛ, Wɔmma yɛnkɔ Dotan. Na Josef tsiiw no nuanom, na okohun hɔn wɔ Dotan.

ref: Jacob loved Joseph more than he did any of his other sons, because Joseph was born when Jacob was very old. Jacob had even given Joseph a fancy coat
text: Afei nna Israel dɔ Josef sen ne mba nyinara, osiandɛ ɔno nye no nkɔdaaber mu ba: na ɔyɛɛ asaasaaba atar fɛfɛɛfɛw bi maa no.

ref: which showed that Joseph was his favorite son, and so Joseph's brothers hated him and would not be friendly to him.
text: Na no nuanom no hun dɛ hɔn egya dɔ no sen hɔn nyinara no wɔtaan no, na woenntum annkasa abodwee do annkyerɛ no.

ref: One day, Joseph told his brothers what he had dreamed, and they hated him even more.
text: Na Josef soo daa, na ɔkaa kyerɛɛ no nuanom no na nkyii wɔtaan no kɛse.

ref: Joseph said, “Let me tell you about my dream.
text: Na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Meserɛ hom, hom ntsie daa a maso yi:

ref: We were out in the field, tying up bundles of wheat. Suddenly my bundle stood up, and your bundles gathered around and bowed down to it.”
text: na hwɛ, nna yɛrekyekyer ewifua ekuwekuw wɔ haban mu, na, hwɛ, mo kuw no soɛree, na ogyinaa tsentsen; na, hwɛ, hom ekuw no botwaa me dze no ho hyiae, na wɔbɔɔ hɔn mu adze maa no.

ref: His brothers asked, “Do you really think you are going to be king and rule over us?” Now they hated Joseph more than ever because of what he had said about his dream.
text: Na no nuanom bisaa no dɛ, Nna tserɛ ampaara dɛ ibedzi hɛn do hen nye no? Ana tserɛ ampaara dɛ ibenya hɛn do tum nye no? Na nkyii wɔtaan no kɛse wɔ n’adaaso no nye ne nsɛm no ntsi.

ref: Joseph later had another dream, and he told his brothers, “Listen to what else I dreamed. The sun, the moon, and eleven stars bowed down to me.”
text: Na ɔsoo daa kor so, na ɔkaa kyerɛɛ no nuanom, na ɔsee dɛ, Hwɛ, maso daa bio; na, hwɛ, ewia nye bosoom nye nworaba dubiako robɔ hɔn mu adze ma me.

ref: When he told his father about this dream, his father became angry and said, “What's that supposed to mean? Are your mother and I and your brothers all going to come and bow down to you?”
text: Na ɔkaa kyerɛɛ n’egya nye no nuanom; na n’egya twuuw n’enyim, na ɔsee no dɛ, Ebɛn daa na aso yi? Nna tserɛ ampaara dɛ emi nye wo na nye wo nuanom bɛba abɔbɔ hɛn mu adze wɔ famu ama wo nye no?

ref: Joseph's brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept wondering about the dream. Joseph Is Sold and Taken to Egypt
text: Na no nuanom enyi beer no do; na n’egya dze ɔdze asɛm no too ne tsirmu. Israel Soma Josef Kɔ No Nuanom Nkyɛn

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: Na no nuanom kɔree kɔhwɛɛ hɔn egya no nguan wɔ Sekem.

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.
text: Na Israel see Josef dɛ, Ana wo nuanom nnyɛn mbowa wɔ Sekem bi a? Bra ma monsoma wo hɔn hɔ. Na ɔsee no dɛ, Emi nye yi.

ref: His father said, “Go and find out how your brothers and the sheep are doing. Then come back and let me know.” So he sent him from Hebron Valley. Joseph was near Shechem
text: Na Israel see no dɛ, Afei kɔ, kɔhwɛ dɛ wo nuanom, nye nguan no hɔn ho ye; na san bɛka kyerɛ me. Nna ofi Hebron bɔnsa mu somaa no ma ɔbaa Sekem.

ref: and wandering through the fields, when a man asked, “What are you looking for?”
text: Na banyin bi hun no dɛ, orikyin haban no mu: na banyin no bisaa no dɛ, Erohwehwɛ ebɛnadze?

ref: Joseph answered, “I'm looking for my brothers who are watching the sheep. Can you tell me where they are?”
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Morohwehwɛ mo nuanom: meserɛ wo ara, kyerɛ me mbrɛ wɔwɔ rohwɛ nguan no.

ref: But before he got there, they saw him coming and made plans to kill him.
text: Na no nuanom no hun no ekyirkyiikyir, na ansaana ɔrebɛn hɔn no, wɔkɔr ne tsir do apam dɛ woboku no.

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Na wɔsesee hɔnho dɛ, Wɔnhwɛ, ɔdaasofo no na ɔreba no.

ref: Reuben heard this and tried to protect Joseph from them. “Let's not kill him,” he said.
text: Na Reuben tsee, na ɔgyee no wɔ hɔn nsamu; na ɔsee dɛ, Mma wɔmma yennku no;

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.
text: Mma hom nnka bɔgya: wɔmfa no nto sar do ha mona yi mu, na mma wɔmmfa nsa nnka no: ɔreyɛ ama nkyɛ ɔaagye no wɔ hɔn nsamu dze no asan akɛma n’egya.

ref: When Joseph came to his brothers, they pulled off his fancy coat
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, Josef dur no nuanom no nkyɛn no, wɔpɔɔr, asaasaaba atar fɛɛfɛw a ɔhyɛ no no

ref: As Joseph's brothers sat down to eat, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead. Their camels were loaded with all kinds of spices that they were taking to Egypt.
text: Na wɔtsenaa famu dɛ woridzidzi no wɔmaa hɔn enyiwa do na wohun Ismaelfo akwantufo kuw bi dɛ wofi Gilead reba, wɔnye hɔn efumpɔnkɔ a wɔsoso eduhuam nye ngo nye mɛɛr dze rokɔ Egypt.

ref: So Judah said, “What will we gain if we kill our brother and hide his body?
text: Na Juda see no nuanom no dɛ, Sɛ yeku hɛn nua na yesiw no bɔgyaa do a, ebɛn mfaso na ɔwɔ mu?

ref: Let's sell him to the Ishmaelites and not harm him. After all, he is our brother.” And the others agreed.
text: Hom mbra ma yɛntɔn no mma Ismaelfo yi, na mma wɔmma yɛmmfa hɛn nsa nnka no; osiandɛ hɛn nua a, hɛn bɔgyaa a. Na no nuanom no tsiee no.

ref: When the Midianite merchants came by, Joseph's brothers took him out of the well, and for
text: Nna Midianfo eguadzifo bi rutwa mu; na woyii Josef fii mona no mu, na wɔtɔɔn no maa Ismaelfo no gyee ndwetɛbona eduonu. Na wɔdze Josef kɔr Egypt.

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Na ber a Reuben sanee baa mona no do no; hwɛ, nna Josef nnyi mona no mu; na osunsuaan ne ntar mu.

ref: Then he went back to his brothers and said, “The boy is gone! What am I going to do?”
text: Na ɔsanee kɔr no nuanom no nkyɛn, na ɔsee dɛ, Abofra no nnyi hɔ; na iyi mara merefa hen?

ref: Joseph's brothers killed a goat and dipped Joseph's fancy coat in its blood.
text: Na wɔfaa Josef n’atar no, na wokum apɔnkyenyin, na wɔdze atar no bɔɔ bɔgya no mu;

ref: After this, they took the coat to their father and said, “We found this! Look at it carefully and see if it belongs to your son.”
text: na wɔdze asaasaaba atar fɛfɛɛfɛw no somae ma wɔdze kɛmaa hɔn egya, kɛsee no dɛ, Adze a yeehu nye yi: hwɛ kyerɛ dɛ wo ba n’atar a.

ref: Jacob knew it was Joseph's coat and said, “It's my son's coat! Joseph has been torn to pieces and eaten by some wild animal.”
text: Na ɔhwɛe, na ɔsee dɛ, Me ba n’atar a; abowa bɔn bi na ɔakyer no; tsetsew na wɔatsetsew Josef no mu.

ref: Jacob mourned for Joseph a long time, and to show his sorrow he tore his clothes and wore sackcloth.
text: Na Jakob sunsuaan n’atar mu, na ɔdze kɔben tam bɔɔ n’asen, na osuu ne ba nda pii.

ref: All of Jacob's children came to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted. “No,” he said, “I will go to my grave, mourning for my son.” So Jacob kept on grieving.
text: Na ne mbabanyin nye ne mbabaa nyinara bae dɛ wɔrebɛkyekye no werɛ; naaso oenntsie ma wɔannkyekye no werɛ; na ɔsee dɛ, Mibedzi yaw ara dze akɔ me ba nkyɛn wɔ samanadze. Na n’egya suu no.

ref: Meanwhile, the Midianites had sold Joseph in Egypt to a man named Potiphar, who was the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Na Midianfo no kɔtɔɔn no wɔ Egypt maa Potifar, Farao no somfo bi a ɔno nye abrafohen no.

ref: About that time Judah left his brothers in the hill country and went to live near his friend Hirah in the town of Adullam.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, aber no ara mu na Juda fii no nuanom nkyɛn kɔree, na ɔkɛtsenaa Adullamnyi bi a ne dzin dze Hira ne nkyɛn.

ref: While there he met the daughter of Shua, a Canaanite man. Judah married her,
text: Na Juda hun Keenannyi bi a ne dzin dze Sua ne babaa bi wɔ hɔ; na ɔwaar no, na ɔnye no dae.

ref: and they had three sons. He named the first one Er;
text: Na onyinsenii, na ɔwoo babanyin; na ɔfrɛɛ no Er.

ref: she named the next one Onan.
text: Na onyinseen bio, na ɔwoo babanyin; na ɔfrɛɛ no Onan.

ref: The third one was born when Judah was in Chezib, and she named him Shelah.
text: Na ɔwoo babanyin bio, na ɔfrɛɛ no Sela: na aber a ɔwoo no no nna ɔwɔ Kezib.

ref: Later, Judah chose Tamar as a wife for Er, his oldest son.
text: Na Juda gyee yer maa n’abakan Er, na ne dzin dze Tamar.

ref: But Er was very evil, and the Lord took his life.
text: Na Juda n’abakan Er yɛɛ bɔn wɔ EWURADZE enyim; na EWURADZE kum no.

ref: So Judah told Onan, “It's your duty to marry Tamar and have a child for your brother.”
text: Na Juda see Onan dɛ, Kɔ wo nua ne yer nkyɛn, na fa no kunabaa, na wo mba ma wo nua.

ref: Onan knew the child would not be his, and when he had sex with Tamar, he made sure that she would not get pregnant.
text: Na Onan nyim dɛ mba no nnkɛyɛ nedze; na ɔbaa dɛ, aber biara a ɔkɔr no nua ne yer nkyɛn no, ɔmaa no ho nsu no guu famu, amma ɔaamma no nua mba.

ref: The Lord wasn't pleased with Onan and took his life too.
text: Na dza ɔyɛe no yɛ bɔn wɔ EWURADZE enyim: na okum no so.

ref: Judah did not want the same thing to happen to his son Shelah, and he told Tamar, “Go home to your father and live there as a widow until my son Shelah is grown.” So Tamar went to live with her father.
text: Nna Juda see n’asew Tamar dɛ, Tsena ekunayɛ mu wɔ w’egya fie kɛpem dɛ me ba Sela benyin: osuro dɛ, bɛyɛ na no so oewu dɛ no nuanom no. Na Tamar kɔree na ɔkɛtsenaa n’egya fie.

ref: Some years later Judah's wife died, and he mourned for her. He then went with his friend Hirah to the town of Timnah, where his sheep were being sheared.
text: Na ɔwɔ hɔ wɔ hɔ nna Juda ne yer a ɔyɛ Sua ne babaa no, owui. Na Juda no werɛ kyekyeree no, ɔkɔr no nguansusuafo nkyɛn wɔ Timna, ɔno nye ne nyɛnko Adullamnyi Hira.

ref: Tamar found out that her father-in-law Judah was going to Timnah to shear his sheep.
text: Na wɔkaa kyerɛɛ Tamar dɛ, Hwɛ, w’asew rokɔ Timna ekosusua no nguan ho nhwi.

ref: She also realized that Shelah was now a grown man, but she had not been allowed to marry him. So she decided to dress in something other than her widow's clothes and to cover her face with a veil. After this, she sat outside the town of Enaim on the road to Timnah.
text: Na oyii no kuna ntar wɔ no ho, na ɔdze ne nkataenyim kataa n’enyim, na ɔkataa noho kɛtsenaa Enaim abotsenano, wɔ Timna kwan nkyɛn; osiandɛ ohun dɛ Sela enyin, naaso Juda ammfa no amma no awar.

ref: When Judah came along, he did not recognize her because of the veil. He thought she was a prostitute
text: Na Juda hun no no, obuu no dɛ edwaman a, osiandɛ ɔakata n’enyim.

ref: “One of my young goats,” he answered. “What will you give me to keep until you send the goat?” she asked.
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Mebɛma wɔdze apɔnkyeba fi mbowa no mu abrɛ wo. Na obisaa no dɛ, Ebɛma me ahobadze bi ma ɔdze ekodu dɛ ebɛma wɔdze aba?

ref: “What do you want?” he asked in return. “The ring on that cord around your neck,” was her reply. “I also want the special walking stick you have with you.” He gave them to her, they slept together, and she became pregnant.
text: Na Juda bisaa no dɛ, Ebɛn ahobadze na memfa mma wo? Na ɔsee dɛ, Wo nsɔwanokaba nye w’ahoma, nye wo poma a okitsa wo nsamu no. Na Juda dze maa no, na ɔnye no dae, na ɔnye no nyinsenii.

ref: After returning home, Tamar took off the veil and dressed in her widow's clothes again.
text: Na Tamar soɛree kɔree, na oyii ne nkataenyim no wɔ no ho, na ɔfaa no kuna ntar hyɛe.

ref: Judah asked his friend Hirah take a goat to the woman, so he could get back the ring and walking stick, but she wasn't there.
text: Na Juda dze apɔnkyeba no maa ne nyɛnko Adullamnyi no, dɛ ɔmfa nkɛgye ahobadze no wɔ ɔbaa no nsa mu: naaso oennhu no.

ref: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, “Where is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?” “There's never been one here,” they answered.
text: Nna obisaa no kuromfo no dɛ, edwaman a ɔtse Enaim kwan nkyɛn no wɔ hen? Na wɔsee dɛ, Edwaman biara mmbaa ha.

ref: Hirah went back and told Judah, “I couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.”
text: Na ɔsanee baa Juda hɔ, na ɔbɛsee dɛ, Mennhu no; na eso ekuromfo no see dɛ, Edwaman biara mmbaa ha.

ref: “If you couldn't find her, we'll just let her keep the things I gave her,” Judah answered. “And we'd better forget about the goat, or else we'll look like fools.”
text: Na Juda see dɛ, Ma ɔmfa, ɔno bohu na hɛn enyim egu ase: hwɛ, memaa wɔdze apɔnkyeba yi kɔree, na ennhu no.

ref: About three months later someone told Judah, “Your daughter-in-law Tamar has behaved like a prostitute, and now she's pregnant!” “Drag her out of town and burn her to death!” Judah shouted.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ odzii bɛyɛ abosoom ebiasa n’ekyir no, wɔkaa kyerɛɛ Juda dɛ, W’asew Tamar abɔ edwaman, na hwɛ, ɔnam n’edwaman mu enyinsen so. Na Juda see dɛ, Hom nkɛfa no mbra ma wɔnhyew no.

ref: As Tamar was being dragged off, she sent someone to tell her father-in-law, “The man who gave me this ring, this cord, and this walking stick is the one who got me pregnant.”
text: Na wɔkɛfaa no bae no, nna Tamar somae ma wɔkɛsee n’asew no dɛ, banyin a ndzɛmba yi yɛ nedze no, ɔno na menye no nyinsen: ntsi, Meserɛ wo, hwehwɛ nyia ndzɛmba yi yɛ nedze, nsɔwanokaba, nye ahoma, nye poma yi.

ref: “Those are mine!” Judah admitted. “She's a better person than I am, because I broke my promise to let her marry my son Shelah.” After this, Judah never slept with her again.
text: Na Juda hun dɛ noara nedze a, na ɔsee dɛ, ɔtsen sen me; susuampa dɛ mammfa no amma me ba Sela ntsi. Na ɔnye no annda bio.

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a ɔrowo no, nna kor dze ne nsa puei; na awogyefo no faa ahoma memen dze kyekyeer ne nsa dɛ, Oyi na odzii kan puei.

ref: At once his hand went back in, and the other child was born first. The woman then said, “What an opening you've made for yourself!” So they named the baby Perez.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, ɔtwee ne nsa kɔdɔɔ mu, na, hwɛ, no nua puei; na ɔwogyefo no see dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na atsew mu aba yi? Dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Perez.

ref: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.
text: Na n’ekyir no na nyia ahoma memen kyekyer ne nsa no bae: na wɔfrɛɛ no Zera.

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Na wɔdze Josef kɔr Egypt, na Potifar, Farao no somfo a ɔyɛ n’abrafohen no, tɔɔ no wɔ Ismaelfo a wɔdze no kɔr hɔ no hɔn nsamu.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: Na no wura hun dɛ EWURADZE nye no nam, na EWURADZE maa dza ɔyɛɛ no nyinara yɛɛ prɔmprɔm wɔ ne nsamu.

ref: Potiphar liked Joseph and made him his personal assistant, putting him in charge of his house and all of his property.
text: Na Josef nyaa n’enyim enyimnyam, na ɔsoom no: na ɔyɛɛ no panyin wɔ ne fie, na ɔdze dza ɔwɔ nyinara hyɛɛ ne nsa.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Na ɔdze dza ɔwɔ nyinara hyɛɛ Josef ne nsa; nna noara nnyim dza ɔwɔ no hɔ gyedɛ edziban a ɔnoara dzi. Nna Josef no ho yɛ fɛw, na n’enyim wɔ nyam.

ref: and Potiphar's wife soon noticed him. She asked him to make love to her,
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ iyinom ekyir no, no wura ne yer no dze n’enyi sii Josef do; na ɔsee dɛ, Enye me nda.

ref: but he refused and said, “My master isn't worried about anything in his house, because he has placed me in charge of everything he owns.
text: Na ɔpowee, na ɔsee no wura ne yer no dɛ, Hwɛ, mo wura nnyim dza ɔwɔ mo hɔ wɔ fie ha, na ɔdze dza ɔwɔ nyinara ahyɛ me nsa;

ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
text: obiara nnyɛ kɛse nnkyɛn me wɔ fie ha; nkyii so ɔnnkam me biribiara ka wo ho, osiandɛ eyɛ ne yer: na ɔbɛyɛ dɛn na mebɛyɛ emumuyɛ kɛse yi, na mayɛ bɔn etsia Nyankopɔn?

ref: She kept begging Joseph day after day, but he refused to do what she wanted or even to go near her.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, daa daa nyinara ɔkaa kyerɛɛ Josef, naaso ɔanngye no pen, dɛ ɔnye no bɛda, anaa ɔnye no bɛyɛ biribi.

ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber yi mu no ɔkɔr fie hɔ dɛ ɔrekɛyɛ n’edwuma; nna fie no mu nyimpa no mu biara nnyi hɔ

ref: Potiphar's wife grabbed hold of his coat and said, “Make love to me!” Joseph ran out of the house, leaving his coat there in her hands.
text: na basia no suoo n’atar mu, dɛ, Enye me nda: na Josef gyaa n’atar no wɔ ne nsamu, na oguanee puei.

ref: she called in her servants and said, “Look! This Hebrew has come just to make fools of us. He tried to rape me, but I screamed for help.
text: ɔfrɛfrɛɛ no nkorɔfo a wɔwɔ ne fie no, na ɔkaa kyerɛɛ hɔn dɛ, Wɔnhwɛ, ɔakɛfa Hebriwnyi aba ma oribesi hɛn atwetwe; ɔbaa me nkyɛn dɛ ɔnye me rebɛda, na medze ndze kɛse kekaa mu:

ref: And when he heard me scream, he ran out of the house, leaving his coat with me.”
text: na ɔbaa dɛ aber a ɔtsee dɛ memaa me ndze do na mekekaa mu no, nna ɔdaan n’atar gyaa me nkyɛn, na oguanee puei.

ref: Potiphar's wife kept Joseph's coat until her husband came home.
text: Na ɔdze n’atar no guu ne nkyɛn ara ma okodur dɛ no wura baa fie.

ref: Then she said, “That Hebrew slave of yours tried to rape me!
text: Na ɔkaa no dɛmara kyerɛɛ no dɛ, Akowaa Hebriwnyi a edze no brɛɛ hɛn no, ɔbaa me nkyɛn dɛ orubogu mo ho fi

ref: But when I screamed for help, he left his coat and ran out of the house.”
text: na ɔbaa dɛ, memaa me ndze do, na mekekaa mu no, nna ɔdaan n’atar gyaa me nkyɛn, na oguanee puei.

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: Na Josef no wura faa no dze no kɔtoo efiadze, beebi mbrɛ ɔhen n’adaduafo gu no: na ɔdaa efiadze hɔ.

ref: the Lord helped him and was good to him. He even made the jailer like Joseph so much that
text: Na EWURADZE dzii Josef n’ekyir, na ɔdoom no, na ɔmaa onyaa efiadzehwɛfo no n’enyim enyimnyam.

ref: he put him in charge of the other prisoners and of everything that was done in the jail.
text: Na efiadzehwɛfo no dze adaduafo a wɔwɔ efiadze hɔ no nyinara hyɛɛ Josef ne nsa; na biribiara a wɔyɛɛ no wɔ hɔ no, ɔnoara na ɔhwɛe.

ref: The jailer did not worry about anything, because the Lord was with Joseph and made him successful in all that he did.
text: Efiadzehwɛfo no annhaw no ho wɔ dza ɔdze hyɛɛ ne nsa mu no mu biara ho, osiandɛ EWURADZE nye no nam; na biribiara a ɔyɛe no, EWURADZE maa ɔyɛɛ prɔmprɔm.

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: Na ɔdze hɔn koguu efiadze wɔ abrafohen no ne fie, wɔ efiadze beebi mbrɛ Josef da no no mu.

ref: They spent a long time in prison, and the official in charge of the palace guard, made Joseph their servant.
text: Na abrafohen no dze hɔn hyɛɛ Josef ne nsa, na ɔsoom hɔn: na wɔkyɛɛr efiadze hɔ mber kakra.

ref: One night each of the two men had a dream, but their dreams had different meanings.
text: Na hɔn beenu nyina sosoo daa, anafua da kor a ɔsor obiara ne daa, nye ne nkyerɛasee.

ref: The next morning, when Joseph went to see the men, he could tell they were upset,
text: Na Josef baa hɔn nkyɛn anapa no, obohun hɔn dɛ wɔabombɔm.

ref: “We each had a dream last night,” they answered, “and there is no one to tell us what they mean.” Joseph replied, “Doesn't God know the meaning of dreams? Now tell me what you dreamed.”
text: Na wɔsee no dɛ, Yɛasoso daa, naaso yennya obi ma ɔnnkyerɛ hɛn ase. Na Josef see hɔn dɛ, Ana nnyɛ Nyankopɔn na ɔma nkyerɛase bi a? Meserɛ hom, hom nka nkyerɛ me.

ref: The king's personal servant told Joseph, “In my dream I saw a vine
text: Na nsahyɛfo panyin no kaa n’adaaso kyerɛɛ Josef, na ɔsee no dɛ, M’adaaso mu, hwɛ, wenyindua si m’enyim;

ref: with three branches. As soon as it budded, it blossomed, and its grapes became ripe.
text: na nkorbata ebiasa wɔ wenyindua no ho: na ɔyɛ dɛ ɔafefɛw, na ɔabɔ nhyiren; na no do nsiansiaw asow wenyinamba a ɔaber:

ref: I held the king's cup and squeezed the grapes into it, then I gave the cup to the king.”
text: na Farao no kɔɔpow kitsa me nsamu, na metsetseew wenyinamba no, na mikyii guu Farao no kɔɔpow mu, na medze kɔɔpow no hyɛɛ Farao ne nsa.

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
text: Na Josef see no dɛ, Ne nkyerɛase nye yi; nkorbata ebiasa no ɔyɛ ndaansa.

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: Aka ndaansa ma Farao eyi wo efi ha na ɔama ekedzi wo dzibew bio: na edze Farao no kɔɔpow bɛhyɛ ne nsa, dɛ nkyɛ kan no a eyɛ ne nsahyɛfo no.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Na sɛ inya beebi si a, ɔno kaa me, na yɛ me ayamuyie, meserɛ wo, na bɔ me dzin kyerɛ Farao, na beyi me fi dan yi mu:

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: na owia na wowiaa me wɔ Hebriwfo asaase do: na ha so mennyɛɛ biribiara a ɔsɛ dɛ wɔdze me to efiadze.

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Na aber a paanootofo panyin no hun dɛ nkyerɛase no ye no, ɔsee Josef dɛ, Mo so m’adaaso mu no, muhun dɛ paanoo fufuw nkɛntsɛn ebiasa sisi m’etsifi:

ref: The top basket was full of all kinds of baked things for the king, but birds were eating them.”
text: na kɛntsɛn a odzi sor no adɔkɔdɔkɔdze a wɔto ma Farao nyinara bi wɔ mu; na nnoma ridzi no kɛntsɛn no mu wɔ m’etsifi.

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three baskets are three days,
text: Na Josef buae na ɔsee dɛ, Ne nkyerɛase nye yi: nkɛntsɛn ebiasa no yɛ ndaansa:

ref: and in three days the king will cut off your head. He will hang your body on a pole, and birds will come and peck at it.
text: na aka ndaansa ma Farao eyi wo tsir wɔ wo do, na wɔasɛn wo wɔ dua do ma nnoma edzi wo nam efi wo ho.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ ne ndaansa no a ɔyɛ Farao n’awoda no, ɔtoo pon maa no nkowaa nyinara: na oyii nsahyɛfo panyin no nye paanootofo panyin no fii efiadze dze hɔn baa no nkowaa no enyim.

ref: He put the personal servant back in his old job
text: Na ɔmaa nsahyɛfo panyin no sanee yɛɛ ne nsahyɛdwuma no bio; na ɔdze kɔɔpow no hyɛɛ Farao ne nsa:

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: na paanootofo panyin no dze, ɔsɛɛn no: dɛ mbrɛ Josef kyerɛɛ hɔn ase no ara.

ref: but the king's personal servant completely forgot about Joseph.
text: Naaso nsahyɛfo panyin no annkaa Josef, na no werɛ fiir no.

ref: Two years later the king of Egypt dreamed he was standing beside the Nile River.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ mfe ebien ekyir no, Farao soo daa: na ohun dɛ nna ogyina esutsen Nael no ho.

ref: Suddenly, seven fat, healthy cows came up from the river and started eating grass along the bank.
text: Na ohun dɛ anantwiber esuon a hɔn ho yɛ fɛw na wɔadɔ seradze, fi esutsen no mu puei; na wobedzidzii wɔ sar no do.

ref: The king went back to sleep and had another dream. This time seven full heads of grain were growing on a single stalk.
text: Na ɔdae na ɔsoo daa bio: na ohun dɛ, eburow mbɛtsem esuon efifir eburonan kor do, amaama na oye.

ref: Later, seven other heads of grain appeared, but they were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
text: Na hwɛ, mbɛtsem esuon a ɔyɛ nkɔkɔr na boka mframa enyinyam no fifirii dzii hɔn ekyir.
